section: 4.3.3 Configuring the product image and bead paths
text:
Configuration mode description / Product overview

The position data, such as time in [ms] for the position, is then shown below the image
navigator. Now switch back to the inspection and continue with the Image navigator 55 to
the start position in [ms] of the bead - the value corresponds to the start position from the
first area created.

Then move the red dot using the slider beneath the product image until it indicates the
start position of the glue application in the product image. Finally, click the Add position
button. The position link just created for this point appears in the link table.

2. Proceed in the same way with the end point. Take the time data from the last area and
switch back to the inspection list.

Use the navigator to move to the end of the image sequence and then move the slider
below the product image to the end of the track.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.3.3 Configuring the product image and bead paths

section: 4.3.3 Configuring the product image and bead paths
text:
Add another position, this time the end position.

The inspection track is linked to the image sequence. If the glue bead is very long or if
you want to increase the accuracy of the display (e.g. in curves as a result of different
robot speeds), it is recommended to add further links at critical points in the glue bead
between the start and end positions (next to drilled holes, lock areas, etc.). To do this, go
to the corresponding position in the adhesive application using the image navigator, then
move the red dot again using the slider until it indicates the selected position in the
component image and click Add position.

Tip - Synchronization
If you want to test the links, move forward or back in the image sequence using the
image navigator. If synchronization is successful, the red dot in the product image
moves accordingly.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.3.3 Configuring the product image and bead paths

section: 4.3.3 Configuring the product image and bead paths
text:
Note
The links created here have no bearing on the inspection accuracy. They are
provided purely to show the errors in the error map in automatic mode. The
errors are then shown with a red dot at the bead position.

If you want to delete a link, select the corresponding entry in the Links table and then
select the Delete position button.

You will find further information on this in:

Navigating in image sequences 55
Product overview 40

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

4.4 Inspections

4.4.1 General

During an inspection, the glue bead is parameterized and the inspection tolerances
are adjusted.

Here you can:

Make general settings, for example define the permitted gap length for the
individual inspection.

Define and create exposures

Create and edit areas

Create and edit references

Create contours and define details with polygons

Operating manual RTVision.t 61
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.1 General

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

4.4.2 Control elements

Please note that for parameterization in RTVision.t, the system must be already fully
functional. Robot speed, glue control and travel paths must be installed as defined in
the production conditions so that final parameterization of the products can take place
in RTVision.t. If the robot speed is changed, for example, following parameterization,
the product must be taught in again, re-parameterized if necessary and the error map
adapted to the new start positions of the areas.

Fig.: The inspection overview in the client shows the list of inspections with product image and inspection-
specific setting options

Add
To create a new inspection, click Add. A new entry with a default name in the defined local
language appears in the list of inspections.

text:
Unlike products, inspections are not numbered via the robot controller. They are
always executed in the order in which they appear in the list of inspections. More
information here 65 .

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

Use this button if you want to duplicate an inspection. The copied inspection is initially given a
default name (this can be changed).

Load
Use this button to load an inspection from a specific inspection file. See also "Save".

Save
Inspections are normally saved together with all the other image processing parameters in a
common file with the extension .appl. However, an inspection can also be saved to a
separate file with the extension .insp using Save. This inspection can then be loaded and
used again later, for example also for a similar product (for more on this, see Directory
structure / files 30 ).

Rename
To rename an inspection, first select the required inspection in the list of inspections. Then
click Rename. Change the name as required.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.2 Control elements

text:

To delete an inspection, first select the required inspection(s) in the list of inspections. Then
click Delete. Answer the confirmation prompt by clicking Yes.

Arrow up / down
Use the two large arrow buttons beneath the list of created applications to change the order of
the applications.

This also changes the order in which the inspections are executed.

Settings
Selecting the button opens the Change error definitions dialog for the marked selection. It
allows global reparameterization for areas, references or polygons that are already available
for inspection.
See Global reparameterization 141

The image sequence associated with the product is required for parameterization at
the inspection level at the latest.
See Loading the image sequence 54

Edit inspection track
Teaches in a new bead path for the selected inspection. See Configuring the product image
and bead paths 56 .

Creates a new link or deletes the currently selected link. See Configuring the product image
and bead paths 56 .

Each inspection is parameterized via the buttons

. See the chapters of the same name.
A transfer to the server can be set up following parameterization or as a precautionary
intermediate step:
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.2 Control elements

text:
Transmit application / Save application
Transfers and saves the current application file loaded in configuration mode (Client) to
automatic mode (server). This button is active automatically when there is a connection
between the client and server.
If the inspection was configured locally in the client, the application cannot be transmitted,
rather it is simply saved with Save. As soon as the connection has been established between
the client and server, the created application can be transmitted. An inconsistency check
verifies prior to transmission whether the application can run in the way it has been created
and provides appropriate information on which configuration steps are missing.

You will find further information on this in:

Switching between automatic mode and configuration mode 29
Inconsistency check 140

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

4.4.4 Auxiliary tools

The inspection overview provides two tools to make your work easier: They can be opened
via the tabs on the right-hand side of the screen and refer in each case to the last run
selected.
·
Manual check
·
Extreme values

Manual check

The manual check greatly simplifies the parameterization of the areas. It can be used to
determine distances and angles (to the nozzle) directly in the individual images in the image
sequence.

To be able to use the auxiliary tool manually, an image sequence must be loaded and
a calibration must be active.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.4 Auxiliary tools

text:
To be able to use the auxiliary tool manually, an image sequence must be loaded and
a calibration must be active.

In the displayed image, you can perform manual checks in all three camera images. This
gives you the option to determine point and distance coordinates.
The point is checked manually with a single mouse click in the image, or the distance by
clicking and recording the end point with the mouse button pressed.
The result is shown beneath Manual check to the right next to the camera images either in
table form or as a profile.
This result is ideally suited for determining the width or brightness of the glue, for example, at
every position. However, distances between the glue bead and the edge of the component
etc. can also be defined quickly. Left-clicking in the image sequence opens a context menu
for further actions (creating areas, references).

The extreme values display makes it much easier to set parameters for areas and optimize

You do not need to start the extreme values display; it is always active.

Configuration mode description / Inspections

4.4.5 Run options

The settings selected in the run options are used for the run each time you navigate through
an image sequence using the image navigator.

Control elements for run options

Operation mode

The operation mode defines the settings used to run the image sequence. The following
options are available:

Test run The glue definitions stored in the individual areas are applied to the image
sequence. The system verifies whether these definitions will permit correct
detection of the edges of the glue bead. The glue bead, nozzle position and the
travel angle are depicted graphically in the image sequence in green, red and
blue. See also:

Visualization in RTVision.t 85
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.5 Run options

text:
Visualization in RTVision.t 85

Teach-in run The glue bead found is taught in when this option is selected. The display in the
image navigator changes. Only the run options "Stop", "Forwards one image"
and "Play image sequence" are available. Reversing is not possible during the
teach-in run.

Optimization The optimization run is effective only in areas where the brightness analysis 91
has been activated. It updates and optimizes the previously taught-in brightness
run

70 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.5 Run options

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

values in the search window. Only the run options "Stop", "Forwards one image"
and "Play image sequence" are available. Reversing is not possible during the
optimization run!

Negative run This test run stops when the glue is found and does not stop if it is not. It is used
for negative control of the glue application and is executed without a prior order. It
is usually executed to complete the parameterization.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.5 Run options

text:
TIP: The inspection run without glue application is more helpful for
optimization; this is also referred to as the negative run. An image
sequence of the bead beforehand when the nozzle was closed (no
glue) is checked in this case. The correct parameterization can
therefore be checked with the inspection run and all deselected
stop options. If the results are red, the parameterization was
successful, since the correct statement that no "glue was found"
is confirmed as an error.

Inspection run The glue definitions stored in the individual areas are applied to the image
sequence. In addition, the calculated values are compared with the taught-in
values as a way of verifying the stored error definitions. The inspection run is
therefore a simulation of an inspection performed in automatic mode. Use the
activation / deactivation options of the different stop options for this purpose.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.5 Run options

text:
After the operation mode is changed, playback automatically starts from the beginning of the
image sequence.

Display options

Reflection filter Shows the reflection filter set in the current area or reference but also for the
contour in the camera images during the run.

The reflection filter is preset by the system when Automatic is selected.

Switching angle Shows the switching angle calculated during calibration in the camera images
(light blue dashed lines).

Stop options
These checkboxes let you automatically interrupt image playback during test / teach-in and
inspection runs. In this way you can be sure that you are not overlooking any errors.
The checkboxes have the following meanings:

Error message Image playback stops if an error occurs in accordance with the tolerance values
set in the current area.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.5 Run options

text:
Error message Image playback stops if an error occurs in accordance with the tolerance values
set in the current area.

Warning message Image playback stops if a warning occurs in accordance with the tolerance
values set in the current area.

Single image Image playback stops if no glue is found in an image. When the standard
error number of sampling points are used, glue must not be found in any of these five
sampling points.

Single check Stops image playback at each reference or contour.

You can resume playback at any time after it stops using the image navigator.

4.4.6 Define inspections

4.4.6.1 General tab

Every inspection generally uses the globally applicable settings from the calibration. On the
General tab, these global settings can be deviated from to a certain extent by making settings
specific to the selected inspection.

All changes concerning the calibration are only recommended under the instruction of
the authorized service provider.

Opening the tab

The general settings can be accessed by double-clicking and opening an inspection in the
inspection list.
If the inspection is opened in this way, the General tab is always active.

Similarly, you can use the button in the parameterization toolbar to select these
settings.
The advantage of this is that you will achieve your objective directly via this toolbar regardless
of the level at which you carry out parameterization in the client.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.6.1 General tab

text:
Controls elements on the General tab

Exposure time
The brightness value for the inspection can also be changed here. This value then applies
regardless of the value that was set globally in the sensor management.
In order to be certain as to which inspection is using which exposure value, this function
should always be activated even if the global and individual values correspond.

Current calibration

Operating manual RTVision.t 73
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.6.1 General tab

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

Changes can be made here if necessary if the calibration has to be modified. They should
really only be pure optimizations, which are also reserved for the proficient expert.

Gap length
Choose Max. length to set the maximum gap length permitted for each inspection.

Note that one level higher for a product you can also set the maximum gap length as
a total of the maximum gaps of the individual inspections.

Repair setting (optional)
The repair function can be selectively activated / deactivated for the selected inspection using
the Repair inspection checkbox.

The repair function is an optional software enhancement and must be enabled
separately. The programming of the communication interface in the higher-level
controller must also be extended.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.6.1 General tab

text:
Inspection length taught-in
The field is green if a length was taught in for the current inspection. In this case, the taught-in
length for the inspection can be found in the Inspection length field. The set tolerance for the
inspection length is shown in the Fixed tolerance field.

Depending on where the software version is installed, the RTVision.t system can
operate in time- or path-based mode. The lengths and also the times can be output
accordingly in ms or in mm. The time- or path-based methodology must be stated
from the outset. A subsequent change should be avoided as this would entail
extensive changes subsequently both on the part of the robot controller (only KUKA)
and in the image processing system.

Default values

List entry A specific data record can be assigned to the inspection from a list of created
default values.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.6.1 General tab

text:

default values.

If this record (when the record is created) is assigned to a specific area of an
inspection, a meaningful name will also have been entered when the record was
created. The latter renames the active areas for the entire inspection with the
Adjust area Adjust area names button with automatic numbering. The name is specified or
names defined in the Default values dialog - at the time it is created.

You will find further information on this in:

Creating and changing default values 156

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

4.4.6.2 Inspection length

The inspection length is set in order to detect a bead path that is too long or too short.

The output is in [ms] as standard, but can also be specified in [mm] depending on the
specific customer interface definition. The time- or path-based methodology must be
stated from the outset, and must therefore be agreed in advance with the
manufacturer. Any subsequent change should be avoided as this would result in
extensive changes subsequently both on the part of the robot controller and in the
image processing system.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.6.2 Inspection length

text:
The time required to teach in an image sequence is recorded for each inspection. The teach-
in process should therefore only be performed when all areas have been created and
parameterized for the complete bead. A value is entered for the inspection length at the end of
the teach-in process, which includes the full length of the glue bead. A system error
(ERROR) will be output if the length of an inspection differs significantly from the length of the
taught-in inspection.

The maximum permitted deviation from the taught-in length can be specified by means of a
parameter in a system file.
This is taken into account as a Fixed tolerance in the length of the inspection.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.6.2 Inspection length

text:
Values in the system file are system values that may only be changed in consultation
with an authorized service engineer. To do this, the application must be closed and
reopened, which is possible only during a break in production.

Only the first inspection with an incorrect length is indicated, even if there are multiple
incorrect inspections. This is because if a system error (ERROR) occurs, the rest of
the bead is not checked.

An error message is output in automatic mode. This is displayed in the error information
window. Only one error message is ever written. If the length of an inspection was identified
as incorrect, the entire inspection is indicated in red in the component image.

You will find further information on this in:

Specific interface extension for path-based image processing as a special specification 237

4.4.7 Exposures

4.4.7.1 General information about exposures

Exposure control is necessary if workpieces made from different materials (e.g. Usibor,
galvanized sheet metal) with different exposure durations are to be inspected. This makes it
possible to control the exposure time of the cameras and therefore the brightness within an
inspection.
The exposure can also be changed or optimized within the inspection in a specific area of the
inspection, since both can be interlinked by means of an identical start position.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.7.1 General information about exposures

text:
By default, the setting is specified in [ms], but can also be specified in [mm]
depending on the specific customer interface definition.The time- or path-based
methodology must be stated from the outset, and must therefore be agreed in
advance with the manufacturer. Any subsequent change should be avoided as this
would result in extensive changes subsequently both on the part of the robot
controller and in the image processing system.

Opening the tab

Access the exposures by double-clicking and opening an inspection in the inspection list and
clicking the vertical Exposures tab on the left-hand side of the screen.

Similarly, you can use the button in the parameterization toolbar to select these
settings.
The advantage of this is that you will achieve your objective directly via this toolbar regardless
of the level at which you carry out parameterization in the client.

4.4.7.2 Settings for exposures

The parameters for an exposure are defined in this dialog.

Exposure time
The brightness of the exposure profile can be defined using the slider or entered directly. The
brightness is controlled by the exposure time of the cameras and applies to the inspection.

Start position
Determines at what time in milliseconds or at which position in millimeters the exposure
should be changed. The specification refers to the value specified in the Image navigator 55 .

Exposure active
Activates / deactivates the exposure. If the separately created exposure is inactive, the global
settings for the exposure are used.

OK
Click OK to save actions / changes made and close the window.

Cancel
Click Cancel to discard actions / changes made and close the window.

Operating manual RTVision.t 79
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.7.2 Settings for exposures

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

4.4.8 Areas

4.4.8.1 General

Areas are used to divide an inspection into any number of sections. This may be necessary,
for example, due to different glue bead widths or changed light conditions. The error tolerance
of an area should not be set too large so that deviations are detected in good time.

Types of areas

A distinction is made between two different types of areas in RTVision.t :

1. Active areas: in addition to the general Area definitions 99 they contain all Search
parameters 103 and Error definitions 108 that are needed to inspect a glue bead. Multiple areas
can be created within an inspection so that different inspection parameters, such as also
varying exposures, can be set.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.1 General

text:
2. Inactive areas: are used to exclude parts of the glue bead from the inspection or to
exclude active areas completely. Inspection parameters do not need to be created for
inactive areas since the glue application is not inspected in these areas. If the inactive area
at the end of the bead is replaced by a checking method, a contour can be used at this point
if necessary.

Open the Areas tab

You access the areas by switching to an inspection and clicking the vertical Areas tab on the
left in the picture.

Similarly, you can use the button in the parameterization toolbar to select these
settings.
The advantage of this is that you will achieve your objective directly via this toolbar regardless
of the level at which you carry out parameterization in the client.

80 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8

using this button must be parameterized manually.

name (this can be changed).

Name column in the list of areas

Position column in the list of areas

4.4.8.2 Create areas

4.4.8.2.1 Standard method

For easier detection, glue bead detection is split into areas.
Each inspection can work with several areas. These include gaps in the glue seam / glue
bead as well as different widths of sections along the bead.
Properties can thus be configured individually to suit the circumstances.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.2.1 Standard method

text:
To do this, select the respective area and click Settings to open the Area name configuration
dialog.

Note - Beware of making settings for multiple areas
If several areas are marked, the button leads to global reparameterization. This
ignores individually parameterized areas and overwrites all error definitions.

The method described here assumes that the two edges of a glue bead can always be
properly detected (edge pair detection).
If edges cannot be detected uniquely, under certain circumstances, the method described in
section Creating areas by means of a brightness analysis 91 may be used.

1. Switch to the Areas tab. The image sequence loaded in the previous step can still be
used. No area has been created yet.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.2.1 Standard method

pressed.

4.4.8.2.2 Areas context menu

The context menu below opens when you manually determine the inspection track in the
image sequence by left-clicking.

Menu commands

Create area
An area is created in accordance with the marking selected in the image sequence.
The start position of the area can be defined by selecting the Move button in the Move start
positions dialog.

Copy area
If at least one area already exists, an additional area can be created from the image
sequence (by left-clicking the image sequence reached via the navigator) via the context
menu as a copy with identical data for the basic, glue and error definitions.

Inactive area
An inactive area can be created here. Use the start position that then has to be defined to
move the inactive area to the required position.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.2.2 Areas context menu

text:
RTVision.t performs an inline check while the sealant is being applied, but the
sensor only records the image sequences directly following application of the
glue. The use of inactive areas allows the controlled inspection of a glue seam
through to the end of the bead or the recording of gaps without checks being
made.
If you want to work with active areas at the end of the seam, define the
parameters here with Contours 132 .

section: 4.4.8.2.3 Graphic display during inline glue inspection
text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

4.4.8.2.3 Graphic display during inline glue inspection

The graphic displays shown during the inline inspection of the glue have the following
meaning:

Blue circle segments Show the actual search area. The search for glue is restricted to
this segment. At the same time, the number of lines indicates the
number of sampling points used. The default value is five and can
also be set to a higher value across systems, though there is an
upward limit.

If necessary, contact your service partner. Independent
changes in system files lead to exclusion of liability.

Green lines With edge sampling, show the calculated glue width of the
respective circle segment. Without the green circle, they visually
display the line lengths of the brightness analysis.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.2.3 Graphic display during inline glue inspection

section: 4.4.8.2.3 Graphic display during inline glue inspection
text:
Yellow line Displays the calculated glue width and also indicates via the
position of the line whether the width was determined diagonally or
straight (perpendicular to the running direction). This orientation is
important for the curve inspection in order to assess the
effectiveness of the algorithm used. The less slanted, the smaller
the deviation from the track width.

Red lines The green lines described above turn red if the specified error /
warning limit values are exceeded.

Green circle With five sampling points, the circle shows the average width of the
glue width determined in the five circle segments.

You will find further information on this in:

Edge detection 89
Brightness analysis 91
Visualization of glue bead 85

88 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.2.3 Graphic display during inline glue inspection

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

4.4.8.2.4 Edge detection

A search window is positioned in the camera image over the point where the glue bead
should be. The system will only search for the glue bead within this window. The information
about the position of the inspection track is acquired from the previous image or at the start of
the bead from the user's specifications (see section Search parameters 103 ).
Each camera has a search angle of 120° available before the camera image changes to the
next camera. The search range is always within the 120° of a camera.
This approach is possible since the high image frequency means that there are very few
differences between two successive images. Since RTVision.t inspects the entire area
around the nozzle, the actual search area is shaped like a circle segment (see dark blue
circle segments with the individual sampling lines).
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.2.4 Edge detection

text:
Fig.: Detection of the glue bead (five sampling points)

For RTVision.t a glue bead essentially consists of a pair of edges. The transition from light to
dark (or vice versa) between two image regions is referred to as the edge. Therefore, when
searching for a glue bead / seam, the system is actually searching for two edges with inverse
light / dark transitions.
During the search, all the edges that are detected are evaluated via rating functions 103 with
certain probabilities.
The glue bead is formed by the two edges with the highest probability rating.
The width and position of the glue bead is then measured.

Operating manual RTVision.t 89
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.2.4 Edge detection

text: Configuration mode description / Inspections
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.2.4 Edge detection

text:
Tip
The default values for the glue definition can be used very effectively during standard
checks for locating the glue bead. It is only if deviations arise because of shadowing or
reflections that the default values for edge contrast or glue brightness should be defined
more narrowly. The aim always is to keep the green circle on the glue bead. The error
messages are not triggered by the search parameters, rather via the defined error
definition values. The error definition is checked by the inspection run performed after
the test and teach-in run.
If the message "Glue not found" is issued, although glue exists, then the values for the
search parameters have generally been defined too restrictively. Make sure in particular
with edge contrast and glue width not to align too strictly with the values determined in
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.2.4 Edge detection

text:
with edge contrast and glue width not to align too strictly with the values determined in
the test run, since any values calculated only reflect the mean values for both edges,
with no separate consideration of the left or right edge.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.2.4 Edge detection

text:
This is performed five times (standard) per image using five sampling points. The lengths of
any interruptions in the glue bead are also recorded.

There is an option to activate a pure Brightness analysis 91 area by area on the basis
of edge searching and detection. The glue or sealant bead in such an area is only
defined via the gray level, i.e. it is not possible to measure the width in this case. The
brightness analysis can be carried out alternatively to or also in addition to edge
detection.

The wealth of information acquired about a glue bead enables RTVision.t to rate with a high
degree of accuracy whether the glue bead is OK or not NOK. The main criteria for an OK
rating are:
·
No interruptions (or a defined interruption length must not be exceeded)
·
Glue bead width within predefined tolerances
·
Glue bead position within predefined tolerances
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.2.4 Edge detection

text:
You will find further information on this in:

Settings for areas 80
Create areas 83
Visualization and interpretation of the seam in the image sequence 85

90 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8

section: 4.4.8.2.5 Creating areas by means of a brightness analysis
text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

4.4.8.2.5 Creating areas by means of a brightness analysis

Under certain circumstances it may happen that the glue is applied in such a way that the
inspection system cannot detect the edges.

As an alternative to the edge detection method described in the previous section, the option
exists to perform a gray level-based brightness analysis in designated areas. This makes it
possible to inspect glue beads even without detecting edges. This checking method is useful
for checking the continuity of the applied bead even in places where there are no discernible
bead edges (e.g. edge "merges" with component edge).

It does, however, require a difference in contrast between the glue bead and the
component surface under the glue bead (i.e. the brightness analysis is not suitable
for "black on black").
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.2.5 Creating areas by means of a brightness analysis

section: 4.4.8.2.5 Creating areas by means of a brightness analysis
text:
In such an area, the glue bead is only defined using the gray level, i.e. it is not possible to
check the width and position of glue bead here. For that reason, the inspection is limited to a
continuity check.

Detection of the glue bead by means of a brightness analysis

A brightness analysis can then be activated area by area here so that the glue bead can at
least be checked for continuity.

1. To teach in the "edgeless" area, a glue bead must first be recorded, which is covered at
the problematic location with a material that contrasts with the glue in a clearly
distinguishable way.

2. These images are parameterized as before using the standard method 83 .

Operating manual RTVision.t 91
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.2.5 Creating areas by means of a brightness analysis

section: 4.4.8.2.5 Creating areas by means of a brightness analysis
text:
[TABLE]
|  | It does, however, require a difference in contrast between the glue bead and the
component surface under the glue bead (i.e. the brightness analysis is not suitable
for "black on black"). |
| --- | --- |
[/TABLE]
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.2.5 Creating areas by means of a brightness analysis

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

3. An area where the brightness analysis is activated is then created at the problematic
location (specification of the positive and negative brightness deviation in gray levels %).
0% stands for black, 100% for white.

4. The application parameterized in this way is taught in.

5. The cover must then be removed before a second recording run.

6. An optimization run is performed with the second image sequence (see also Run
options 70 ). The taught-in values are optimized for the actual conditions. As a result of the
first run, the system knows where the glue bead should be. The taught-in values are
optimized in the background and this not obvious to the user. It is only with the inspection
run that it is obvious that the brightness analysis is being used due to the change in the
green detection circle for the square.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.2.6 Additional method (Brightness analysis)

text:
The brightness analysis can be used only if there is a visible contrast between the
glue bead and the component surface under the glue bead (i.e. the brightness
analysis is not suitable for "black on black").

You will find further information on this in:

Settings for areas 80
Areas context menu 86
Teaching in areas 113

4.4.8.2.6 Additional method (Brightness analysis)

In addition to the alternative Brightness analysis checking method, it can also be used as an
additional method in certain cases.
As an additional method, the brightness analysis can also be used for edge pair or single
edge detection.

This is useful if material or glue deviations occur occasionally in the glue bead which means
that edge pair or single edge detection is not exact enough.
With the additional brightness analysis, a defined measured width can be inspected via gray
level comparison.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.2.6 Additional method (Brightness analysis)

text:
Edge pair detection + brightness analysis combination

With this combination, edge pair detection is carried out first and, if glue is detected, a
brightness analysis is also carried out. The settings for this are made for each area in the
Area name dialog. Activate Additional method (Brightness analysis) after selecting Edge
pair detection from the Checking method field.

92 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.2.6 Additional method (Brightness analysis)

text:
[TABLE]
|  | The brightness analysis can be used only if there is a visible contrast between the
glue bead and the component surface under the glue bead (i.e. the brightness
analysis is not suitable for "black on black"). |
| --- | --- |
[/TABLE]
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.2.6 Additional method (Brightness analysis)

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

The comparison is carried out on the glue width that is detected. If the comparison is
unsuccessful, the glue is classified as "not detected". As with the brightness analysis, the
brightness limits can be set here and also the maximum error rate on the Error definitions
tab.
The brightness limits can be entered as relative percentages (in relation to gray level 0%
=black and 100%=white) or as absolute values.

Example of relative and absolute with an assumed average gray level of 60%:

Relative limits with +/- 20% to 60%

Absolute limits with 40% and 80% absolute to 60%

For a Detailed analysis, a Maximum error rate is entered as a % that does not relate to the
gray level % but to the number of results with a pixel-based review of the results.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.2.6 Additional method (Brightness analysis)

text:
Example: per pixel the result is considered OK or NOK. If, for example, with 50 pixels at one
point, 11 pixels are NOK, with an error rate of 20%, the overall result is NOK.
In other words, the number of OK/NOK results per pixel is considered and this is used to
calculate an error rate. This then produces the overall result OK or NOK.
This error rate fine-tunes the evaluation even more.

Single edge detection and brightness analysis combination

Operating manual RTVision.t 93
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.2.6 Additional method (Brightness analysis)

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

In contrast to edge pair detection with brightness analysis, in this checking method the user
defines the measured width for the brightness analysis.
With single edge detection, a clear edge can be seen only on one side, the other side is, for
example, the end of the component or the glue and material have the same color and do not
create a contrast. By defining the inspection width, the inspection can be carried out
successfully in the subsequent brightness analysis.
Hence, the combination of single edge detection and brightness analysis is almost ideal in
most cases because it is the most precise checking method.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.2.6 Additional method (Brightness analysis)

text:
On the Basic definitions tab, the Measured width field is available when brightness analysis
is used as an additional method..
The measured width defined corresponds to the length of the green sampling lines.
The measurement direction is defined additionally by the combination of Glue appearance
and Edge transition.

94 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.2.6 Additional method (Brightness analysis)

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

4.4.8.2.7 Glue gap

In practice, the glue definition or edge pair detection can also be used for gap variance rating.
Gap variance rating is simply inverse edge pair detection.
This means that in the basic definition, the glue appearance defines what is or is not glue.
If glue is detected, it is classified as "detected". Initially as a single image error, from a certain
length it becomes a warning / error.
The difference to edge pair detection is that here a fixed measuring start angle is defined and
does not have to be taught in.

Tip
The start angle can also be determined easily from the image sequence by using the
auxiliary tool.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.2.7 Glue gap

text:
Tip
The start angle can also be determined easily from the image sequence by using the
auxiliary tool.

Go to the starting point in the image sequence, calculate the bead width with the auxiliary
tools and right-click Create area in the context menu to open the dialog shown including the
value determined for the start angle. This can also be used to determine the start angle at the
start of a glue gap.

This angle is then valid for the whole area. If the start angle changes over time, several areas
should be used to cover the entire section.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.2.7 Glue gap

text:
With step seams, these gaps may not always be the same length.
So as not to generate pseudo errors, inactive areas that are as large (long) as the glue
process and signal inaccuracy should be used for the glue-gap and gap-glue transitions. To
compensate for signal inaccuracy, a synchronization reference (special mode) should be
used at the start of the inspection.

You will find further information on this in:

Create areas 83
Auxiliary tools 68
Synchronization reference 129

Operating manual RTVision.t 95
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.2.7 Glue gap

auxiliary tool. |

[/TABLE]

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

4.4.8.3 Settings for areas

The settings for areas are divided into the following elements:
·
General parameters that are always present 96 in the Area name dialog with the basic
settings, the image sequence display with sampling points, navigator area and table
values for manual checks, extreme values and test values.

Tabs to be selected

·
Basic definitions 99
·
Search parameters 103
·
Error definitions 108
·
Profile 109
·
Repair 235 (optional)

The repair function is an optional licensed software enhancement and must be
enabled separately. The programming of the communication interface in the higher-
level controller must also be extended. The Repair tab is visible only if the repair
function is enabled.

4.4.8.3.1 General parameters
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.3.1 General parameters

text:
4.4.8.3.1 General parameters

Mark the area for which you want to set parameters. Now click the Settings button or open
the Area name dialog by double-clicking the area name.

Note - Marking multiple areas will result in global reparameterization
Note that the Settings button will only lead to the area dialog if a single area is
marked. If several areas are marked, this leads to global reparameterization.

The general parameters (basic settings) can be found in the Area name dialog in the lower
part of the screen and on the right-hand side of the dialog.
They are always included in the dialog (framed in red), regardless of which tab was selected
to parameterize the glue bead.

text:
Camera view with sampling points and navigator
Navigator control and the display of the camera view with sampling points refer to the image
sequence currently loaded for the inspection.
Messages such as "Glue not found" or similar are displayed below the navigator field.

Operating manual RTVision.t 97
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.3.1 General parameters

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

Basic settings
If the Profile tab is activated, the basic settings represent the only option for changing the
area parameters. The reason for this is that the Profile tab should only be regarded as a
parameterization aid and does not actively intervene in parameterization.

Start position
Determines at what time in milliseconds or at which position in millimeters the area starts.
The specification refers to the value specified in the Image navigator 55 . If the area is created
interactively by marking with the cursor, the start position is defined and applied automatically.

Area activated
Checkbox activates / deactivates the area. The name is also indicated next to the area for
better orientation.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.3.1 General parameters

text:
The settings cannot be changed if an area is inactive. An area can also be created as an
active or inactive area interactively from the context menu by marking with the cursor.

An area that is active does not necessarily have to be already taught in. Information
about the teach-in status is shown in column T., whereas the active / inactive
indicator is shown by a green or gray lamp in front of the name of the area. If the lamp
in column T. is red, the required teach-in run of an area marked as active is missing.

The glue application is not inspected in inactive areas. Inactive areas are shown by a
gray lamp in front of their name and in column T. They allow the image sequence to
be recorded smoothly up to the end of the bead since they are defined, but not
checked, 2-3 cm beyond the end of the bead.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.3.1 General parameters

text:
Angle knob
Defines the angle for the glue search in degrees around the applicator nozzle. If the area is
created interactively by marking with the cursor, the angle is applied automatically.

Arrow keys
The arrow keys can also be used to scroll within the area list. Switching quickly between the
areas of an inspection with the same screen layout is thus helpful for quickly checking
parameters.
The display of the last selected tab remains, but with customized content for each area.

OK
Click OK to save actions / changes made and close the window.

Cancel
Click Cancel to discard actions / changes made and close the window.

You will find further information on this in:

Loading image sequences and using the navigator 54

4.4.8.3.2 Basic definitions

The basic parameters for the selected area are defined in the Area name window.

Start angle
Shows the start angle to the nozzle.
The current value is displayed simultaneously in the table of test values. If you want to apply

the current angle, just select the "smaller" icon next to the Angle to nozzle field.
Note that only the angle of the first image may be adopted for the start angle.
A manual attempt elsewhere leads to this message:

Operating manual RTVision.t 99
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.3.2 Basic definitions

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

The start angle can also be determined easily from the image sequence by using
the auxiliary tool Manual check.

Go to the starting point in the image sequence, calculate the bead width with the auxiliary
tools and right-click Create area in the context menu to open the dialog shown above
including the value determined for the start angle. A new area was created at the same time.
Name assignment and further parameterization can follow.

See also section Create areas 83 e.g. standard method or Auxiliary tools 68

Five sampling points
The standard number of sampling points is set to five and algorithmically adjusted to improve
the evaluation reliability of the system.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.3.2 Basic definitions

text:
Sampling distance
The distance between the individual sampling points can be defined. This can improve the
evaluation of the bead in certain radii. The maximum possible distance between sampling
points is 2.0 mm. The sampling distance is depicted by the distance between the green lines
(5 lines) derived from blue circles of evaluation on the image sequence. The smaller the
distance, the more precise the scan.

Fig.: Graphical depiction of the sampling distance with five sampling points

Camera selection
Specify whether the software should select the camera automatically or whether a specific
camera should be used. Normally, this option can be left in automatic mode.

When using a specific camera, the system will not switch to another camera if the
glue crosses the switching threshold between the cameras.

hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.3.2 Basic definitions

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

Checking methods

Edge pair detection
The glue bead is delimited here by an edge pair. The limit values are defined by the
parameters on the Search parameters and Error definitions tabs.
By activating the Curve inspection checkbox, the system uses a different algorithm in
curves to optimize implementation of the width determination.
Edge pair detection can be further improved by marking the Additional method (Brightness
analysis) checkbox.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.3.2 Basic definitions

text:
Single edge detection
If this checking method is selected, you can restrict the inspection to one edge of a glue bead
in the relevant area. The selection of the edge to be inspected (left or right) is dependent on
the setting under Edge transition on the Search parameters tab. Single edge detection can
also be further improved by marking the Additional method (Brightness analysis)
checkbox.
The single edge detection is useful when the glue bead is close to another seam and only the
removed side forms a clear contrast with the background.

A width inspection is not possible with this setting.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.3.2 Basic definitions

text:
A width inspection is not possible with this setting.

Brightness analysis
This can be carried out as a stand-alone checking method or in combination with single edge
detection or edge pair detection as an additional method.
For details, refer to section Additional method (Brightness analysis) 92 .
As an alternative to edge detection, it is also possible to carry out a pure gray level-based
brightness analysis. For details, refer to section Brightness analysis 91 .
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.3.2 Basic definitions

text:
Gap inspection
Gap inspection allows inspection of places on the components that must contain no glue so
that they can be processed further. A typical application case is the inspection of step seams,
although this can also be covered with inactive areas and the standard methods.
In the case of active areas, but with the selective checking method Gap inspection, a NOK
message is output if glue is detected in the sampling area. For details, refer to section Glue
gap 95
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.3.2 Basic definitions

text:
Reflection filter
The reflection filter can be used to minimize reflections on the glue bead and the sheet metal.
The stronger the reflections that occur, the higher the set value should be. If the reflection
filter is preset to Off, no reflections are eliminated. The reflection filter can be optionally
activated in the run options (see section Run options 70 ). The effect is most obvious with an
activated filter in individual step mode of the currently active area. If you change the filter
value, for example, between 2 and 50, in the window above, the filter effect is interactively
visible in the displayed image.
The filter size defines the number of pixels that are grouped depending on the filter type. The
larger the value, the lower the reflection, but also the lower the edge detection.

Operating manual RTVision.t 101
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.3.2 Basic definitions

[/TABLE]
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.3.2 Basic definitions

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

The pixel values can be processed with three different filter types:

Automatic The system automatically selects the appropriate filter type for
processing.

Open (Opening Filter) Bright interfering points on dark surfaces are removed.

Close (Closing Filter) Dark interferences on a light background are overwritten by bright
pixels.

With a high filter value (max. 50), even actual edges in the image become blurred. In
the current image sequence, the filter effect can be observed directly and can be
selected appropriately.

Glue appearance
For clear differentiation in relation to the gray value analysis, you have to specify whether the
glue in question is a dark glue on a bright background or a bright glue on a dark background.
The immediate effect on the image sequence can be observed in the same dialog. This
should always be used.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.3.2 Basic definitions

text:
If you change to Glue brighter as background, remember to also change the
quality settings.

Edge transition
Here you can specify which edge transition should be used (from bright to dark or vice
versa).

This option should only be set additionally when Single edge detection is selected.

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

4.4.8.3.3 Glue model

RTVision.t uses rating functions to identify the pair of edges corresponding to the glue bead
from the multitude found in the image. These rating functions 103 are described as the "glue
model".
The settings for the rating functions for every inspection are made in the respective area in
Area name window on the Search parameters tab.

The glue model defines the possible properties of the glue in the respective area. This is
necessary so that the software can select the correct edge pair belonging to the glue from the
multitude found in the image. For example, it defines which widths could qualify as glue.

If, for example, different application speeds result in the glue width within an area increasing
from 3 mm to 6 mm, the glue model will define the entire range between 3 mm and 6 mm as
a possible glue width.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.3.4 Search parameters

text:
The maximum and minimum values are shown in the Extreme values display 69 .

4.4.8.3.4 Search parameters

The parameters or the rating functions for glue variance, glue width, edge contrast and glue
brightness for glue searches are defined on the Search parameters tab in the Area name
window.
The software needs these parameters to distinguish the glue and/or the glue edges from a
component edge, for example.
Accordingly, the rating functions differ according to the checking methodology. Thus, with
single edge detection, only the position and the contrast are to be evaluated.

In order to reliably find the glue bead from the multitude of edge pairs visible in an image, the
system first has to identify all the edge pairs in order to weight them using the above-
mentioned rating functions. The basic principle is described in section Inspecting the glue
bead 89 .
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.3.4 Search parameters

text:
The weighting takes place in the four rating functions, using four support points in each case.
The position of these support points as well as the rating at the support points can be
changed. The software calculates an overall quality rating from the four rating functions for
each edge pair. This overall quality rating can be seen in the screen area on the left in the
client during the teach-in run.
These support points can be defined with two different methods:

Simple definition with symmetrical data structure

The default includes the simple method using tolerances that are to be defined with a value in
plus/minus direction.
This definition method is the tool for unique glue quality, clear edges and symmetrical data
structure.

Operating manual RTVision.t 103
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.3.4 Search parameters

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

In the case of symmetrical rating functions, in particular, this simplified definition can be
used especially on the basis of numeric values as a definition tool.
Variations with the same value are possible for each parameter with +/-. A value difference
between + and - does not apply here. Equally, the quality rating also cannot be influenced.

Detailed definition for experts (optional)

Note - System file settings
If necessary, contact your service partner. Independent changes in system
files lead to exclusion of liability.
Once you have decided on the detailed definition, it is no longer possible to switch to
the simple method as the symmetry of the parameter settings has generally been
canceled. Working in parallel with both methods is not possible within one system.

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

With up to 32 parameters that can be set, this definition type is significantly more complex.
It is the ideal tool for the complex glue bead and for experts in parameter evaluation. It offers
greater scope for parameterization including quality variance.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.3.4 Search parameters

text:
To edit the rating functions, default values are already provided by the system, which are
displayed when creating the area.
A small interactive default setting can be made if the area was created manually with the
mouse (left mouse button) from the image sequence via the context menu Create area.
The values for contrast, width and brightness are then already preset and can be adjusted
simply to the specifications.
In terms of position variance, the default value from the system applies, since this rating
function cannot be determined interactively. It is also necessary to make manual corrections
here.

Another possibility to find default values when new areas are being created is to first assign
previously created default data records for each inspection. Even then, parameters must be
set depending on the test values and specifications.

Operating manual RTVision.t 105
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.3.4 Search parameters

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

Glue variance rating - Find position
Rates the position of the glue. For example, the system presets the values stated below,
which must be adjusted manually depending on the test values / extreme values.

Edge contrast rating - Find contrast
Rates the edge contrast between the substrate and glue. This value cannot be determined
using the auxiliary tool. Even though a contrast is specified, if it is used then the system is
generally very restricted in its search values and tends to issue the error message "Glue not
found". If faults like shadowing or reflection then arise, delimiting the edge contrast is a
promising adjustment option that can be applied successfully with caution. 100% is the
maximum contrast. See Edge detection 89
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.3.4 Search parameters

text:
Glue width rating - Find width
The width of individual edge pairs is rated here. This makes it possible to exclude edge pairs
that are too far apart. First, determine the actual width of the glue using the auxiliary tool (left
mouse button) and estimate by how much the glue width can vary. These upper and lower
limits should be given a quality rating of 1. All widths above or below this should be assigned a
quality of 0, for example, but intermediate values can also be set.

Example: The glue application should be 3 mm wide. The average width, however, is 2.5 mm
to 3.5mm. This produces the following rating function:

[mm] 2 2.5 3.5 4

Quality 0.0 1.0 1.0 0.0

V. 4.8

Tip - Using auxiliary tools
The actual spread can be estimated using the Extreme values display 68 .
Further details can also be accessed on the Profile tab by left-clicking in the image
sequence.

Glue brightness rating - Find brightness
Rates the brightness of the glue. The value of 0% corresponds to black and 100% to white.
The values in between correspond to the gray level between dark and bright.
This option is visible only when edge pair detection is selected. The latter can be adjusted for
the respective inspection in Area name | Basic definitions | Checking method.

4.4.8.3.5 Error definitions

The error definitions specify which tolerances will result in a warning or a NOK during an
inspection in automatic mode.

Fig.: This error definition is only active if edge pair detection is selected as the checking method.

In order for the system to be able to show not just errors but also warnings, limit values for
both errors and warnings must be entered.

Glue gap Defines the maximum permissible length for a glue gap; any greater gap will
result in an error message or warning message.

Is disabled / hidden accordingly if a gap inspection is selected as the checking
method.

Glue position Defines which variance between the glue bead and nozzle will result in an error
or warning message.

Whether an error or warning is output depends on whether the position variance
exceeds the specified length.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.3.5 Error definitions

text:
Whether an error or warning is output depends on whether the position variance
exceeds the specified length.

Is disabled / hidden if a brightness analysis is selected as the checking method.

Min./max. glue Defines which change (decrease or increase) in the width of the glue bead will
width result in an error message or warning message.

Whether an error or warning is output depends on whether the width change
exceeds the specified length.

To help identify the point at which the system has already calculated contrast and brightness
gradients, this information is displayed at the bottom of the glue profile section.
They refer to the position where the sampling points are evident in the glue bead. For critical
inspections, it may be helpful for further parameterization to look at the individual sampling
points.

Note that although the units of the x-axes are identical, the scaling does not match because
lengths or offset are displayed as different parameters.
In addition, in the standard table 97 (top right), the values determined when manually creating
the profile are entered in the Manual check table.

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

4.4.8.3.7 Identifying and correcting parameterization errors

A test run is recommended after you have created areas. For this test run, all the options
under Stop options should be activated. This ensures that problems caused by e.g.
incorrect parameters will be identified. You should also activate the reflection filter.

Incorrect parameter settings can happen especially when areas are created manually. If this
happens, the glue bead may not be found at all or the wrong width may be determined. If all
the options under Stop options are activated, then the test run will be stopped if the glue is
not found. You can then take corrective action. Incorrectly determined widths will not cause
image playback to stop. This error must be identified by the operator. The corresponding error
messages are displayed below the navigator for this purpose.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.3.7 Identifying and correcting parameterization errors

text:
If the test run stops, you should proceed as described below. Each time you make a change,
test whether it has a positive effect on the problem and whether it might result in any negative
effects somewhere else in the glue bead.
Here is a list of the most common parameterization errors:

·
The area starts in the wrong place

Check the Start position parameter in the Area name window under Basic settings.

The start position can be shifted here directly or via the Areas context menu via a
relative shift in the ms range (+/- entry possible).
·
Playback stops directly at the start of the area

If playback stops directly at the start of the area, check whether the start angle is
correct. You will also find this information in the Area name window on the Basic
definitions tab.
·
No glue found when switching from one area to the next
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.3.7 Identifying and correcting parameterization errors

text:
Check whether the Start angle is correct in the Area name window on the Basic
definitions tab. Test your changes directly in the adjacent image sequence. Use the
navigator here to follow the progress over the glue area and observe the behavior of the
sampling points in the camera view.
·
No glue is found when switching from one camera to the next, the search area is not
above the glue.

Check the search area and modify it if necessary on the Search parameters tab. You
can check your changes at any time using the Test button.
·
No glue is found within an area with one or more sampling points.

Check whether there are reflections on the glue bead. If this is the case, increase the
reflection filter (e.g. between 2 and 40) in the Area name window on the Basic definitions
tab.

If the glue bead merges with any seams or edges, the reflection filter setting is too high.

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

Calculate the width at the current position using the mouse (auxiliary tool). Check
whether this width is covered in the Glue width rating function.

You will find the rating function in the Area name window on the Search parameters tab.
If not, adjust the rating function accordingly.

Calculate the average gray level of the glue at the current position using the mouse
(auxiliary tool). Check whether this gray level is covered in the Glue brightness rating
function.

You will find the rating function in the Area name window on the Search parameters tab.
If not, adjust the rating function accordingly.

If the image looks very fuzzy and the contrast is low, check the Edge contrast rating
function. The edge contrast cannot be determined directly using the auxiliary tool.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.3.7 Identifying and correcting parameterization errors

text:
The extreme values for contrast are, however, logged in the extreme values display.
Check whether the values listed in the extreme values display are covered by the Edge
contrast rating function.

Another useful tool is the additional Profile tab, where you can use the mouse to
determine brightness and contrast gradients in the image sequence.

You will find the rating function in the Area name window on the Search parameters tab.
If not, adjust the rating function accordingly.

You will find further information on this in:

Create areas 83
Search parameters for glue definition 103
Profile tab as auxiliary tool 109 for contrast and brightness gradient
Run options 70
Edge detection 89

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

4.4.8.4 Teaching in areas

To teach in areas, the areas must be created successfully.

Areas are taught in as follows:

1. Switch to configuration mode and select the appropriate product / inspection.

2. Load the required image sequence.

3. Reset the extreme values display by clicking Reset.

4. With the exception of Single check, mark all the boxes on the Run options tab.

5. First start a test run by selecting Test run from the list in Run options | Operation
mode. This is the best way of finding incorrect parameter settings, since image playback
will stop immediately at these points.

6. If the test run completes without any problems, check once again whether the values
displayed in Extreme values are within the values set in the area settings in Glue
definitions.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.4 Teaching in areas

text:
7. If this is the case, select Teach-in run from the list under Run options | Operation
mode to start the teach-in run. Let the image sequence run until the end so that all the
areas are taught in.

Operating manual RTVision.t 113
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.4 Teaching in areas

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

Note
The teach-in run must always be performed, otherwise the inline inspection system
will not be able to inspect any components.

Tip
In the area list within the inspection, check whether all areas of an inspection have been
taught in correctly; the green signal confirms the status after teaching in. If the signal in
the T. column in the list of areas fails to come on, this indicates that the corresponding
area was not taught in; for example, inactive areas are displayed in this way. The signal
turns red if the teach-in run is faulty or has failed.

8. Now start an inspection run to verify the teach-in run. To do this, select Inspection run
from the list under Run options | Operation mode.

As soon as the teach-in run has been selected, the system asks whether the teach-in
run should be started.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.8.4 Teaching in areas

text:
As soon as the teach-in run has been selected, the system asks whether the teach-in
run should be started.

When confirmed, all areas are taught-in immediately.

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

4.4.9 References

4.4.9.1 General

References are used to check the position of a component edge relative to the glue bead at a
defined position. Like areas, references must be created and taught in.

In addition to the existing functionality of references, double references can be used
to determine the distance between two edges, e.g. the distance between a glue bead
and a component edge to the left or right of the glue bead. They can also be used to
determine the distance from the end of a glue bead to a component edge in the
direction of travel of the glue application system (by means of a double reference).
Double references 126 work with a primary reference and the additionally defined
reference as well as the required distance checks.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.9.1 General

text:
A triple reference allows the distance between the component edge and the center of
the glue to be checked with a help reference supplementing the additional reference
(edge distance of a component to the center line of the glue bead).

Synchronization references, or special mode as it is referred to, can be used to avoid
a delayed start trigger by the synchronization references acting as special references
for an inspection. This is done by using the position of a synchronization reference to
compare the calculated distance and the distance actually covered.

Opening the tab
To access the list of references, go to an inspection, double-click to open it and click the
vertical References tab on the right-hand side of the screen.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.9.1 General

text:
Similarly, you can use the References button in the parameterization toolbar to select
these settings.
The advantage of this is that you will achieve your objective directly via this toolbar regardless
of the level at which you carry out parameterization in the client.

Move the mouse pointer to the camera view image that shows a reference and the glue bead.
Select the component edge and left-click to choose Create reference in the context menu.

Control elements
Add
If no reference exists as yet, the system prompts to create a new reference graphically using
the mouse.

Use the navigator to go to a suitable position in the image sequence and mark a possible
reference with the left mouse button (component edge).

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

Select Create reference in the context menu with the left mouse button.

Copy
Use this button if you want to duplicate a reference. The copied reference is initially given a
default name (this can be changed).

Load
To load a reference, click Load. A selection window then opens. Click the file to be loaded
followed by Open.

Save
To save a reference, click Save. A selection window then opens. Assign a name and click
Save.

Delete
To delete a reference, first select the relevant reference(s) in the list of references. Then click
Delete. Answer the confirmation prompt by clicking Yes.

Rename
To rename a reference, first select the required reference in the list of references. Then click
Rename. Change the name as required.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 4 Configuration mode description > 4.4.9.1 General

text:
Move
Opens the Move start positions dialog to set the start position of the active or inactive
reference precisely. If the start position of several references is to be changed, mark them in
the list and all marked references will be moved by the same value in one step by clicking
Move.

Settings
Opens the Reference name window. Click the relevant reference in the list of references
followed by Settings. Double-clicking the reference also opens the Reference name
window.
If you select several references, you will be taken to the Global reparameterization 141 dialog
where you can overwrite the previously valid error definitions for all marked references, even
after individual parameterization.

V. 4.8

Start
Clicking the Start button skips the image sequence to the image where the reference was
created.

Test
Tests the properties stored in the selected reference in the current image.

Note - Start and Test
To test the reference in the image where it was created, first click the Start button.

C. column in the list of references
Marks the currently displayed reference with an arrow.

Name column in the list of references
The green indicator shows whether this reference is active. The name of the reference is also
shown here.

Position column in the list of references
Specifies (in ms or mm) the position of a reference within an inspection.

T. column in the list of references
The light indicates whether this reference has been taught in.

Displayed values
The values shown under Contrast, Variance, Distance, Difference and Time offset
correspond to the values determined in the current image.

4.4.9.2.1 General parameters

Note - Marking multiple areas will result in global reparameterization
Note that the Settings button will only lead to the Reference dialog if a single
reference is marked. If several references are marked, this leads to global
reparameterization.

The general parameters (teach-in settings) can be found in the Reference name dialog in the
lower part of the screen and on the right-hand side of the dialog.
They are always included in the dialog, regardless of which tab was selected to parameterize
the reference (framed in red).

Start position
Determines at what time in milliseconds or at which position in millimeters referencing should
take place. The specification refers to the value specified in the Image navigator 55 .

Reference activated
Activates / deactivates the reference. If a reference is inactive, the definitions for the reference
and errors cannot be changed.

A reference that is active does not necessarily also need to be taught in. This must
be done by means of an additional teach-in run.

text:
Configuration mode description / Inspections

You will find further information on this in section Teaching in references 130 .

Teaching in references quickly
The fully configured reference can already be taught-in after parameterization when the
reference is created. Quick teach-in is not absolutely necessary, since testing is always
carried out with the teach-in run, which takes place after the test run. If this teach-in run has
already taken place and something still needs to be corrected afterwards for a reference, the
quick teach-in phase is preferable for this single reference. The complete teach-in run of the
inspection across all elements is then no longer necessary. Special mode is an exception, as
it can only be taught in via a teach-in run.

Table values differentiate according to actual / reference value

or distance from the center of the bead to the edge (triple reference)

is at the green cross. Transition goes
from dark to light.
