Operating manual RTVision.t 195
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 5 Automatic mode > 5.7 Recording image sequences

text:
Automatic mode / Recording image sequences

The status referred to above for these image sequences is shown in the History dialog in the
last column Acquire state.
The recorded images are saved on the hard disk as soon as image recording ends.
If the inspection system is to be parameterized using the newly recorded images, you will
need to switch to configuration mode.

Tip - Recording image sequences
The function can be selected both in the server and in the client. However, both counter
and execution are always handled by the server, since the image recording initiated on
the client must be transferred to the server prior to execution.

You will find further information on this in:

Acquire state in history 188
Switching between automatic mode and configuration mode 29

text:
Automatic mode / Saving result data

5.8 Saving result data

Saving the result data on the server makes it possible to save not just error data but all the
data recorded by the system during a component inspection to an individual XML file (one
XML file is created per component). This server setting applies globally.

Note - Required for detailed view in the viewer
This setting is mandatory if the viewer is to be used fully to analyze past results. This
default setting is required for the detailed view in the viewer to provide the necessary
data.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 5 Automatic mode > 5.8 Saving result data

text:
Results data can also be changed and saved for each product, with the frequency indicating
which component result (of a product) will be saved.
For example, "10" means that the data of every tenth component of the same product is
saved. The frequency can be set per product on the client, whereas the setting is globally
valid in automatic mode (server).

Fig.: Individual settings per product are made on the client

The storage interval on the server under Settings | Save optional data can be used to
further reduce the volume of data.

If different workpiece types (components/products) are produced and inspected in a station,
in addition to activating result data storage generally in configuration mode (Client), you can
decide whether the respective product should be excluded from the storage of result data.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 5 Automatic mode > 5.8 Saving result data

text:
The following result data can be logged:
·
Width inspection (brightness measurement with activated Brightness analysis 91 )
·
Position variances of the glue bead from references
·
Distance check using double and/or triple references

text:
Automatic mode / Saving result data

To globally activate the storage of results in automatic mode (server), the following steps are
necessary:

1. In automatic mode (server), in the menu bar 177 click Settings | Save optional data.

2. Authorize yourself by entering a password in the Login dialog.

3. Activate the Save results checkbox in the Save optional data window.

4. In Storage interval, specify the data recording interval (in milliseconds) for inspecting
the component.

With values less than 5 ms, all area inspections will be recorded since the system
records an image only every 5 ms (for more on this, see also Image structure /
image sequences 36 ). Please consult the authorized service provider concerning
the relevant data for path-based quality testing.

text:
Automatic mode / User management

5.9 User management

The user manager can be used to create users globally who are assigned a user level.
Each user level is defined with fixed access rights by the system, distinguishing between
"Administrator", "User" and "Disabled".
Access rights are not assigned individually to the levels, but are fixed.
Choose Disabled to block the user. The advantage of this is that the user data is retained, but
the user has no rights.
Access can thus be activated and deactivated for a short time.
Full access exists for the administrator level, while the user is often restricted with respect to
global setting variants.

Calling up the user manager
To launch the user manager, select Settings | Manage users in the menu bar. To be able to
use the user manager, you must have administrator rights in RTVision.t .

Control elements
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 5 Automatic mode > 5.9 User management

text:
Control elements

Add
To create a new user, click Add. A new entry with a default name appears in the list of users.
The new user does not have a password yet. You can rename a user and change the user
level by clicking the Adjust button.

Delete
To delete users, first select the required user(s) in the list of users. Then click Delete.
Answer the confirmation prompt by clicking Yes. The currently logged in user cannot be
deleted and neither can the administrator, even after acknowledging the confirmation prompt.

Operating manual RTVision.t 199
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 5 Automatic mode > 5.9 User management

text:
Automatic mode / User management

Rename
To rename a user, select the required user in the list of users. Then click Rename. Change
the name as required. As a logged-in user, you cannot change your own designation.

Settings
You can modify user properties here. This concerns the assignment of a user level and
changing the password.

Arrow up / down
The arrows can be used to change the order of the users in the list.

Name
To rename a user, first select the required user in the list of users. Then click Settings. You
can edit the user name in the User name selection field
(same function as Rename).

User level
The level determines the rights of the individual users in RTVision.t . To change the level, start
by selecting the required user in the user list. Then click Settings. You can then assign a
level to this user in the User level selection field.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 5 Automatic mode > 5.9 User management

text:
Change password
To set the password for a user, first select the required user. Then click Settings followed by
Change password. You can enter the new password in the dialog shown below. The
password must be entered twice as a precaution. Both entries must be the same, otherwise
an error message will be shown and you will have to enter the password again.

text:
Automatic mode / Switching language

5.10 Switching language

RTVision.t can be delivered in several language versions. The basic languages are German
and English.
If you are missing languages, please contact the service partner. Integration of additional
languages is possible, but must be ordered from the manufacturer as a language package.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 5 Automatic mode > 5.10 Switching language

text:
The languages can be changed at any time during operation, but the switching feature may
be password protected. It is not necessary to restart the program.
To switch to another language, select the required language version from the Language
menu.
The language is switched separately within each RTVision.t module or can also be set at a
higher level.
If the associated optional unlicensed specification is set up, the system can be preset so that
all other modules operate in line with the language setting of the first module opened. The
individual language switch per module is no longer necessary. In addition to the display of the
interface in the local language, the names are assigned automatically when new products,
inspections, areas, references and polygons are created in the set language.

You will find further information on this in:

Special unlicensed specifications 246
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 5 Automatic mode > 5.10 Switching language

Special unlicensed specifications 246

Chapter 6
Results evaluation
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 6 Results evaluation > General

text:
Results evaluation / The viewer

6 Results evaluation

6.1 The viewer

The RTVision.t system logs all relevant result data in the background and saves it in special
xml files on the server for further processing. This data can be loaded and displayed via these
result files. The viewer is a useful enhancement to the system for presenting result data and
its error evaluation in graphical form.

When a component inspection has been completed, select Display history in the View main
menu. The history opens. Double-click a row to open the viewer.

The date, time, product name and result status are shown in the history. If the list is displayed
via the server, double-clicking a line opens the viewer with the selected individual result of the
product.
The results are also automatically sorted by color.

204 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 6 Results evaluation > 6.1 The viewer

text:
Results evaluation / The viewer

Fig.: RTVision.t viewer displays results separately from the list of existing protocols

The RTVision.t viewer module can also be started as a standalone program and load the
desired result report (xml files).
By opening the viewer multiple times, individual reports can be viewed side by side at the
same time.

Operating manual RTVision.t 205
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 6 Results evaluation > 6.1 The viewer

text:
Results evaluation / The viewer

Viewer controls

Load report
To view these results selectively at a later time, open the RTVision.t viewer and choose Load
report.

Detailed view
See Detailed view 207

Round view
If an error message is marked in the error list on the right-hand side, the detected error
location is also displayed in the camera view 175 . It is possible here to switch between the
individual camera view and the 360° view by selecting the Round view button.

Error details
This button is used to show / hide meaningful error symbols for gap, position variance, etc. on
the error map.

You will find further information on this in:
Overview of error icons 278
Switching the camera view 175

206 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 6 Results evaluation > 6.1 The viewer

text:
Results evaluation / Detailed view

6.2 Detailed view

Storage of result data must be activated as a prerequisite for viewing details in the RTVision.t
viewer.
On the RTVision.t server, activate the corresponding function under Settings | Save
optional data.

Tip
With values of under 5 ms, all inspections will be recorded since the system records an
image "only" every 5 ms by default (see also Image structure / image sequences 36 )

If the viewer is opened from the History, individual inspections can be selected. This activates
the Detailed view button. Double-click to open the detailed view. Extensive graphical
evaluation options are provided here, especially for analyzing errors.

Control elements in the detailed view
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 6 Results evaluation > 6.2 Detailed view

text:
Control elements in the detailed view

Errors, warnings and calculated values can be displayed as detailed graphics. The product
overview screen gives users a relatively precise idea of where the problem zones are; a
detailed evaluation can then be carried out precisely via the inspection data calculated.

Select an individual inspection and click Detailed view to evaluate the results further.

Left-click the overview to show and hide individual windows, such as glue gap, glue width,
glue offset, glue brightness or error rate. Separate criteria such as reference gap, reference
offset or reference distance have also been set up for evaluating references.

Operating manual RTVision.t 207
V. 4.8

36 |

hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 6 Results evaluation > 6.2 Detailed view

text:
Results evaluation / Detailed view

This is an example of a detailed view using a glue gap analysis as an example.

Fig.: A glue gap from an inspection from the viewer (determined by means of a brightness analysis) is shown
in detail

The warning is displayed here as soon as it has been taught in as a tolerance, even if it was
not output as a result. The point of the inspection where the transition of a warning to a fault is
exceeded can be seen clearly here. When parameterized accordingly, warning and error
could be detected as separate results. This precise error evaluation allows optimum
parameterization of the system.
For some interpretations, it helps to view the checking method applied at the same time as
the associated error definitions of the inspection on the client.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 6 Results evaluation > 6.2 Detailed view

text:
In the detailed view it is possible to enlarge individual areas by dragging the window with the
left mouse button. Right-click to return to the original view.
Various views can also be displayed via the buttons that can be selected above the view. The
first view after opening always shows a view with all active buttons; if you select these
afterwards, the displays change accordingly.
Some parameters can be enabled / disabled by clicking the name above the view (e.g. error /
warning).

Chapter 7
Communication
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 7 Communication > General

text:
Communication / General

7 Communication

7.1 General

The inspection system RTVision.t must be able to communicate with the robot controller
(hereinafter called the "controller") for the purpose of exchanging various signals and data.

The signals are used, for example, to transmit inspection results, start / stop events or
general system messages (see below for details); the image processing system is always
the secondary system in this case.

Communication with the controller takes place via fieldbus systems (Profibus, DeviceNet,
CANopen) or Industrial Ethernet systems (Profinet, EtherNet/IP, EtherCAT).

The basic interface can be adapted in terms of its communication with existing glue
controllers and additional signals exchanged by extending the interface.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 7 Communication > 7.1 General

text:
Tip - Joint definition of interface
Prior to installing the RTVision.t system, consult with manufacturer in order to define
details of the data transfer jointly in advance.

The sequence of communication from start to finish for RTVision.t is as follows:

text:
Communication / Basic interface

7.2.1 Incoming signals

Product ON / OFF (0.0)

A change from 0 to 1 indicates the start of a product, a change from 1 to 0 ends the current
product.

Inspection ON / OFF (0.1)

A change from 0 to 1 indicates the start of the inspection period, a change from 1 to 0 ends
the current inspection area. Signal changes in this bit will be ignored if no product is selected
(i.e. "Product ON / OFF" is 0).

Change inspection (0.2)

Each signal edge (change from 0 to 1 or 1 to 0) during an inspection period (i.e. "Product
ON / OFF" is 1 and "Inspection ON / OFF" is 1) causes an uninterruptible change to the next
inspection. Signal edges outside of inspection periods (i.e. "Product ON / OFF" is 0 or
"Inspection ON / OFF" is 0) will be ignored.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 7 Communication > 7.2.1 Incoming signals

text:
This function is required if inspection parameters, such as the exposure settings for the
sensor, are to be changed without interruption during application of the glue or sealant.

Product ID (1.0 - 1.7)

The number to select the product (component) is an 8Bit unsigned integer. The number is
read when a product is selected (i.e. "Product ON / OFF" from 0 to 1).

Speed (2.0 - 3.7)

The system receives the speed of the robot as an unsigned integer via bits 2.0 to 3.7. A 0
(zero) corresponds to 0 mm/s and the value 65,535 ((2^16) - 1) corresponds to the maximum
speed that can be transmitted. This can be configured in mm/s. The transmitted data must
use the same multiplier that has also been stored in the system. Configuration takes place in
the file "RTVision_t.ini". This file may only be processed explicitly by the authorized
service provider. The assignment is linear.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 7 Communication > 7.2.1 Incoming signals

text:
Operating manual RTVision.t 213
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 7 Communication > 7.2.1 Incoming signals

text:
Communication / Basic interface

7.2.2 Outgoing signals

Mirrored signals
The mirrored signals indicate which input signals are currently applied to the system.
However, delays can occur between the input signal (e.g. input: Product ON / OFF à 1) and
the mirror (e.g. output: Product ON / OFF à 1). These delays are caused by tasks the system
has to perform first in order to achieve the required status.

The following signals are mirrored:

Product ON / OFF (0.0)
Mirrors the incoming signal "Product ON / OFF".

Inspection ON / OFF (0.1)
Mirrors the incoming signal "Inspection ON / OFF".

Change inspection (0.2)
Mirrors the incoming signal "Change inspection".

Product ID (1.0 - 1.7)
Mirrors the incoming product ID. The product ID is read in at the moment the product is
selected (Product ON / OFF à 1).

214 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 7 Communication > 7.2.2 Outgoing signals

text:
Communication / Basic interface

Status messages

The status messages provide information on the actual, current system status. There can
also be delays between the input signal and the change in status with these signals due to
tasks the system needs to perform.

The following states can be reported:

System ON / OFF (2.0)
Is set to 1 when the vision system is started.

Automatic ON / OFF (2.1)
Is set to 1 when automatic mode is started.

Product selectable (2.2)
Is set to 1 when the system is expecting a product to be selected.

Inspection selectable (2.3)
Is set to 1 when an inspection can be selected.

Inspection changeable (2.4)
Is set to 1 if an inspection can be changed.

Result available (2.5)
Is set to 1 if a product was ended and therefore a result is available (OK, WARN, NOK, ERR).
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 7 Communication > 7.2.2 Outgoing signals

text:
Result available (2.5)
Is set to 1 if a product was ended and therefore a result is available (OK, WARN, NOK, ERR).

Hardware ready (2.6)
The sensor, camera and strobe controller are checked by the system when the program is
started, when the product is selected and also when the sensor is checked manually to
determine if it is correct.
Signal is set to 1 if the hardware status is OK, to zero if one or more components (also cable
connection) is/are faulty.

Result messages:
The system uses the result messages to report the result of the most recent run.

The following results can be reported:

Product result OK / WARN / NOK / ERR (3.0 – 3.3)
If the signal "Result available" is set to 1, the system transmits the corresponding result with
these bits.

Operating manual RTVision.t 215
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 7 Communication > 7.2.2 Outgoing signals

text:
Communication / Basic interface

7.2.3 Programming the controller

Selecting a product

A product may only be selected by the controller if the following signals are set:

Outgoing from RTVision.t (see also section Outgoing signals 214 )
2.0 "System ON / OFF" to 1
2.1 "Automatic ON / OFF" to 1
2.2 "Product selectable" to 1

Outgoing from the controller (see also Incoming signals 213 )
0.0 "Product ON / OFF" to 0
0.1 "Inspection ON / OFF" to 0

Sequence on the controller side

1. Check whether the outgoing signals from RTVision.t (see above) are set correctly.

2. Check whether the outgoing signals from the controller (see above) are set correctly.

3. Send product number to RTVision.t .

4. Set signal "Product ON / OFF" to 1
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 7 Communication > 7.2.3 Programming the controller

text:
3. Send product number to RTVision.t .

4. Set signal "Product ON / OFF" to 1

When the product has been selected, RTVision.t sets the following signals.
1.0-1.7 "Product ID" correspond to the bits of the product number
0.0 “Product ON/OFF” to 1
2.2 "Product selectable" to 0
2.3 "Inspection selectable" to 1

If there is RTVision.t no product with the transmitted product number, the signal
"Inspection selectable" will be left at 0 at the time when "Product ON / OFF" is
mirrored.

If the bit "Inspection ON / OFF" was not reset to 0 before a product was selected, the
signal "Inspection selectable" will be left at 0 even after the product is selected, even if
the product number is valid.

216 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 7 Communication > 7.2.3 Programming the controller

text:
[TABLE]
|  | If there is RTVision.t no product with the transmitted product number, the signal
"Inspection selectable" will be left at 0 at the time when "Product ON / OFF" is
mirrored. |
| --- | --- |
[/TABLE]


[TABLE]

signal "Inspection selectable" will be left at 0 even after the product is selected, even if
the product number is valid. |
| --- | --- |
[/TABLE]
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 7 Communication > 7.2.3 Programming the controller

text:
Communication / Basic interface

Deselecting a product

A product may only be deselected by the controller if the following signals are set:

Outgoing from RTVision.t (see also section Outgoing signals 214 )
2.0 "System ON / OFF" to 1
2.1 "Automatic ON / OFF" to 1
2.2 "Product selectable" to 0
2.3 "Inspection selectable" to 1

Outgoing from the controller (see also Incoming signals 213 )
0.0 “Product ON/OFF” to 1
0.1 "Inspection ON / OFF" to 0

Sequence on the controller side

1. Check whether the outgoing signals from RTVision.t (see above) are set correctly.

2. Check whether the outgoing signals from the controller (see above) are set correctly.

3. Set signal "Product ON / OFF" to 0

When the product has been deselected, RTVision.t sets the following signals.
0.0 "Product ON / OFF" to 0
2.2 "Product selectable" to 1
2.3 "Inspection selectable" to 0
2.5 "Result available" to 1
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 7 Communication > 7.2.3 Programming the controller

text:
Canceling a product

Changing the signal "Product ON / OFF" from 1 to 0 during an inspection run cancels the
product inspection, i.e. the inspection currently in progress is stopped. A system error (ERR)
will be returned in this case.

Operating manual RTVision.t 217
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 7 Communication > 7.2.3 Programming the controller

text:
Communication / Basic interface

Selecting an inspection

The signal "Inspection ON / OFF" may only be set to 1 by the controller if the following signals
are set:

Outgoing from RTVision.t (see also section Outgoing signals 214 )
2.0 "System ON / OFF" to 1
2.1 "Automatic ON / OFF" to 1
2.2 "Product selectable" to 0
2.3 "Inspection selectable" to 1

Outgoing from the controller (see also Incoming signals 213 )
0.0 “Product ON/OFF” to 1
0.1 "Inspection ON / OFF" to 0

Sequence on the controller side

1. Check whether the outgoing signals from RTVision.t (see above) are set correctly.

2. Set signal "Inspection ON / OFF" to 1.

When the inspection has been selected, RTVision.t sets the following signals.
0.1 "Inspection ON / OFF" to 1
2.2 "Product selectable" to 0
2.3 "Inspection selectable" to 0
2.4 "Inspection changeable" to 1

218 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 7 Communication > 7.2.3 Programming the controller

text:
Communication / Basic interface

Deselecting an inspection

The signal "Inspection ON/OFF" may only be set to 0 by the controller if the following signals
are set:

Outgoing from RTVision.t (see also section Outgoing signals 214 )
2.0 "System ON / OFF" to 1
2.1 "Automatic ON / OFF" to 1
2.2 "Product selectable" to 0
2.3 "Inspection selectable" to 0

Outgoing from the controller (see also Incoming signals 213 )
0.0 “Product ON/OFF” to 1
0.1 "Inspection ON / OFF" to 1

Sequence on the controller side

1. Check whether the outgoing signals from RTVision.t (see above) are set correctly.

2. Set signal "Inspection ON / OFF" to 0.

When the inspection has been deselected, RTVision.t sets the following signals.
0.1 "Inspection ON / OFF" to 0
2.2 "Product selectable" to 0
2.3 "Inspection selectable" to 1
2.4 "Inspection changeable" to 0

Operating manual RTVision.t 219
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 7 Communication > 7.2.3 Programming the controller

text:
Communication / Basic interface

Change inspection

The controller may send the signal “Change inspection” only if the following signals are set:

Outgoing from RTVision.t (see also section Outgoing signals 214 )
2.0 "System ON / OFF" to 1
2.1 "Automatic ON / OFF" to 1
2.2 "Product selectable" to 0
2.3 "Inspection selectable" to 0
2.4 "Inspection changeable" to 1

The first inspection of a product must not be started with the signal "Change
inspection" (0.2); instead it must always be started by setting the signal "Inspection
ON / OFF" (0.0) to 1 (from 0).

The last inspection of a product must not be ended with the signal "Change
inspection" (0.2); instead it must always be ended by setting the signal "Inspection
ON / OFF" (0.0) to 0 (from 1).

The signal "Change inspection" (0.2) may only be changed if the signal "Inspection
ON / OFF" (0.0) is set to 1.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 7 Communication > 7.2.3 Programming the controller

text:
The signal "Change inspection" (0.2) may only be changed if the signal "Inspection
ON / OFF" (0.0) is set to 1.

Outgoing from the controller (see also Incoming signals 213 )
0.0 “Product ON/OFF” to 1
0.1 "Inspection ON / OFF" to 1

Sequence on the controller side

1. Check whether the outgoing signals from RTVision.t (see above) are set correctly.

2. Inversion of the signal "Change inspection"

After an inspection has been changed, RTVision.t sets the following signals.
0.1 "Inspection ON / OFF" to 1
2.2 "Product selectable" to 0
2.3 "Inspection selectable" to 0
2.4 "Inspection changeable" to 1

text:
Communication / Basic interface

Retrieval of the results

The result supplied by RTVision.t is only valid if the following signals are set:

Outgoing from RTVision.t (see also section Outgoing signals 214 )
2.0 "System ON / OFF" to 1
2.5 "Result available" to 1

Sequence on the controller side

1. Check whether the outgoing signals from RTVision.t (see above) are set correctly.

2. Read bits 3.0 – 3.3 to query the result.

The controller must continually provide the same robot speed as a prerequisite for the
correct operation of RTVision.t.

text:
Special specifications – optional /

8 Special specifications – optional

RTVision.t offers additional options and functionalities on the basis of the specifications
required in projects.
These are not available by default and in some cases have to be enabled via license. In
addition, they usually require configuration adjustments on the customer side.

For queries regarding activation and necessary configuration changes, please contact the
service partner who supports you.

The following extensions are possible in principle, but are not included in the installation by
default:

·
Transfer of seam numbers to inspections 242

text:
Special specifications – optional / Target check: Configuration and workflow

8.1 Target check: Configuration and workflow
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.1 Target check: Configuration and workflow

text:
For the target check, the complete system and its individual components (from the robot
gripper arm through the dispenser with nozzle to the sensor) are referenced against each
other, i.e. an image recording of the reference target is optimized in configuration mode
(Client) and saved as a reference value in the product or application.
The application configured in this way with a specific product is transmitted to the server,
where it is available for a repeat target check after a predefined product ID is retrieved.
It checks the extent to which the current system status concerning imaging inspection
deviates from a saved target status (reference). This is done using a permanently mounted
reference target, which is approached when called.
One possible application would be, for example, saving a cyclic call of the target check in the
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.1 Target check: Configuration and workflow

text:
One possible application would be, for example, saving a cyclic call of the target check in the
PLC so that the reference and actual position of the system with respect to the reference
target can be determined.
Furthermore, the test program can also be started after every mechanical replacement of a
component (e.g. gripper, robot, sensor, nozzle, etc.) involving changes within the system
and, if the result is NOK, the cause of the error can be determined and the magnitude of the
error can be checked with regard to its relevance.
The settings made are specific for each station, as they reference the robot, sensor, gripper
arm and dispenser with nozzle together as an (overall) system.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.1 Target check: Configuration and workflow

text:
Note
This test program is an optional function of the RTVision.t system and can be
enabled after installation. Significant effort is required for the configuration and has to
be clarified in advance before commissioning.
The functional checkbox in the RTVision.t client is called Compare sensor at product
level.
If necessary, contact the authorized service partner.

Note: Configuration of a reference is NOT the same as sensor calibration. The latter
is carried out in-house by the manufacturer and is permanently saved on the sensor
upon delivery.
Repeat calibration (manual) via RTVision.t is generally not required and is not
intended. The manufacturer accepts no liability for any damage resulting from
independently performed manual calibration of the sensor.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.1 Target check: Configuration and workflow

text:
For configuration of the target check, paths must be programmed (by the plant
manufacturer) and at the same time settings made in the RTVision.tsystem.

For configuration of the target check, a reference image of the reference target is saved for
the current sensor. In the target checks that are then performed automatically, this reference
is checked for current deviations of the nozzle position, which can be set in pixels. For better
orientation, the conversion of the pixel data into millimeters is displayed in an information field,
depending on the calibration of the sensor used.

text:
Special specifications – optional / Target check: Configuration and workflow

Procedure - robot controller
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.1 Target check: Configuration and workflow

text:
Procedure - robot controller

·
The reference target is mounted as close as possible to "Home" and must be
unchangeable for repeated approach of the industrial robot.
·
The robot controller must be programmed so that when a predefined product ID is called,
the center of the nozzle is directed to the center of the reference target, with the nozzle
positioned perpendicular to the plate.
·
The working distance must be maintained with a deviation of +/- 1 mm and the center
points must be exactly above each other.
·
The robot arm rotates the sensor head until the three cameras display an optimal image
of the reference target.
·
Note this in the RTVision.t client in Settings | Manage sensors | Sensor | Settings
(see below). It is considered optimal if all three cameras find and display the circle points
on top of each other.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.1 Target check: Configuration and workflow

text:
When the optimal position is reached, this position must be saved as a fixed target position
before the reference target in the industrial robot.

Note - Observe in the robot controller!
The test program created is called in the RTVision.t server. The predefined product
ID is called with the saved inspection. For communication, the following applies:
product ON, inspection ON, a short wait time must be observed (e.g. <100 ms before
the reference target), the signal for inspection OFF, product OFF then follows. The
robot can then leave the position before the reference target.
The result OK / NOK is output in automatic mode (server).

For the initial configuration described here, additional settings in the RTVision.tclient are
required.

Procedure - settings in the RTVision.tclient in two steps

·
Creation of the product with predefined ID (Client)
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.1 Target check: Configuration and workflow

text:
Procedure - settings in the RTVision.tclient in two steps

·
Creation of the product with predefined ID (Client)

and
·
creation of the reference image - configuration of a reference with subsequent
transmission of the application to the RTVision.tserver.

V. 4.8

the reference target), the signal for inspection OFF, product OFF then follows. The
robot can then leave the position before the reference target.
The result OK / NOK is output in automatic mode (server). |

hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.1 Target check: Configuration and workflow

text:
Special specifications – optional / Target check: Configuration and workflow

Step 1: Procedure - Creating a product with predefined ID
·
Start the RTVision.tserver and client. Select Connect.
·
In the client, create an application with a product, e.g. "reference target" to which you
assign the sensor and the defined product ID (in the example ID=99).

The target check is always linked with the product and its predefined ID via an
inspection defined by the system. Accordingly, this ID may not be used for any
other product inspection, as it does not allow individual configuration of the
inspections.

·
Check the box for Compare sensor and a specific system inspection is created
automatically. To do this, existing inspections must be deleted.

·
When you confirm the confirmation prompt, an inspection is created by the system, which
cannot be edited by the user (and administrator).
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.1 Target check: Configuration and workflow

text:
Special specifications – optional / Target check: Configuration and workflow

Step 2: Procedure - Configuring the reference image

Before the application is transmitted, the reference image is configured. The industrial robot is
moved to the position "Target position" and the reference image is shown in the dialog in the
RTVision.t client under Automatic | Manage sensors | Sensor | Settings as described
above. The sensor can also be accessed via the main menu by selecting Settings | Manage
sensors.

Having linked the sensor, the calibration data for the sensor will already be stored in
the application

To create the reference image, the position in front of the reference target (see description
above) is approached. The correct working distance and centered nozzle position must be
ensured.

·
The actual image is created with Image recording :

Operating manual RTVision.t 229
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.1 Target check: Configuration and workflow

text:
[TABLE]
|  | Having linked the sensor, the calibration data for the sensor will already be stored in
the application |
| --- | --- |
[/TABLE]
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.1 Target check: Configuration and workflow

text:
Special specifications – optional / Target check: Configuration and workflow

·
Then select Configure reference :

The recording of the reference target shown here represents the maximum
possible point distribution, but this is not absolutely necessary in practice. It is
important that the reference image and the image sequence from the inspection
do not differ, or only differ within the set max. deviation [px]. To allow the deviation
to be classified more easily, the specification in [mm] is also displayed, converted
in accordance with the permanently stored calibration data for the sensor. If the
deviation stated in px is exceeded, the check will result in an error NOK.

As soon as the Configuration dialog is opened, the quality of the current image
recording is checked. For optimal display of the pixels, the brightness threshold can be
varied in manual mode.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.1 Target check: Configuration and workflow

text:
The default is that the circle points can already be displayed in automatic mode.

The more circle points in the image are displayed in green and the camera positioning is
shown vertically below each other, the better the reference image settings.

In most cases, it is sufficient to optimize the point search by means of the
brightness threshold so that the default values for the point filtering can be retained.

·
Click OK to accept the reference configuration and then transmit it to the current distance
value of the sensor.

·
To do this, select Reference sensor to open the corresponding dialog and enter the
values Search radius and real Work distance in [mm].

230 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8

hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.1 Target check: Configuration and workflow

text:
Special specifications – optional / Target check: Configuration and workflow

·
Next, define the tolerance value for the permitted maximum offset of the nozzle
position in [px]. This is defined once during configuration and is essential for the result of
the automatic application in the server later on. To allow the deviation to be classified
more easily, the specification in [mm] is displayed underneath, converted in accordance
with the permanently stored calibration data for the sensor.

·
When you exit the dialog by clicking OK, the reference image is automatically saved in
a directory created by the system and subsequently used as a reference for every target
check called.
·
After completion of the configuration in the client, the target application, as with every
other application, can be transmitted to the server.

Result interpretation - check errors!
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.1 Target check: Configuration and workflow

text:
Result interpretation - check errors!

If, for example, deviations with a tolerance of less than 4 px (~1 mm) are acceptable, the
server result is OK for the target check.
If the deviation of the calculated nozzle position is greater than 4 px, the server result after
completion of the inspection would be NOK.
This is generally due to inaccuracy in one of the system components and should be checked
before the start of production.

However, before making corrections to the hardware components, the error message
from the target check should be verified with respect to the severity of the error and
its relevance.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.1 Target check: Configuration and workflow

text:
The error message NOK is output on the server side with no additional information. Here, the
RTVision.tclient offers the option of an analysis or reconfiguration for the target check.
·
If the result is NOK on the server side, load the automatically saved error image
sequence by clicking the Load error image button.
·
By clicking Load reference image, the image originally saved with the reference
configuration can be loaded and viewed again.
·
By clicking the Compare sensor button you can compare the set parameters with the
current reference image and analyze the accurate px result at a glance.

This table shows the position values of the nozzle tip including the calculated deviation in
[px] and thus allows the error relevance to be assessed for an NOK result.

Operating manual RTVision.t 231
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.1 Target check: Configuration and workflow

text:
[TABLE]
|  | However, before making corrections to the hardware components, the error message
from the target check should be verified with respect to the severity of the error and
its relevance. |
| --- | --- |
[/TABLE]
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.1 Target check: Configuration and workflow

text:
Special specifications – optional / Target check: Configuration and workflow

·
If the deviation is below the set max. deviation, you can end the inspection with OK.
·
If the error is acceptable, you can reconfigure the reference by increasing the maximum
deviation [px]. A reference with a new deviation is configured, which then leads to an OK
result as a new reference (image).
·
However, if the maximum deviation is no longer acceptable, the complete system must
be checked for incorrect adjustment of the individual hardware components.

Avoid manual recalibration of the sensor if possible. A negative target check is not
sufficient grounds for manual calibration of the sensor. It only shows deviations with
respect to an original reference. The sensor is not calibrated in this case. A clear
distinction must be made here.

text:
Special specifications – optional / Repair interface (licensed option)

8.2 Repair interface (licensed option)
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.2 Repair interface (licensed option)

text:
The repair function requires extended signal exchange between the inspection system
RTVision.t and the systems involved in the process, such as glue controller, robot controller
and higher level PLC. By way of illustration, these systems should be collectively treated as a
higher-level controller.
Like the inspection itself, the repair is performed on the basis of the product. In addition to
supplying the product result, the inspection system signals to the higher-level controller
whether a part that returned NOK can be repaired. The higher-level controller repeats the
component program (robot path) if repair is required. It also signals to the inspection system
that the most recently inspected component is to be repaired. The inspection system then
provides information about the points on the bead where the applicator nozzle needs to be
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.2 Repair interface (licensed option)

text:
provides information about the points on the bead where the applicator nozzle needs to be
opened for the purpose of reworking the bead; this information is provided for the repair run
as an output signal.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.2 Repair interface (licensed option)

text:
Signals

Bit offset Signal output Signal input

0 Product reparable Repair product

1 Close nozzle

Note
The additional repair signals happen in the same time frame as the signals of the
basic interface PV2.

Product reparable (0)
Whether a repair is possible is output synchronously with the output signal "Result available"
after the first inspection. The signal is reset when the next product is selected. The previous
component can no longer be repaired if no repair is performed with the next product selected.

Repair product (0)
If, when a product is selected, the signal is set and the previous inspection detected a
reparable component, the output signal "Close nozzle" can be used for the repair. In addition,
the product ID for the repair inspection must not change compared with the first inspection.

Close nozzle (1)

Inspection status Value for "Close nozzle"
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.2 Repair interface (licensed option)

text:
Close nozzle (1)

Inspection status Value for "Close nozzle"

Repair mode active, no product 0

Repair mode active, outside inspection 1

Repair mode active, request "Close nozzle" 1

Repair mode active, request "Open nozzle" 0

V. 4.8

| --- | --- | --- |
| 0 | Product reparable | Repair product |
| 1 | Close nozzle |  |
[/TABLE]

basic interface PV2. |

hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.2 Repair interface (licensed option)

text:
Special specifications – optional / Repair interface (licensed option)

Basic settings for fieldbus communication

The repair function is a licensed additional function that can be optionally activated if a license
is available.
The following values are used for the repair function in the RTVision_t.ini configuration file:

[PROTOCOL-V2]
OutputStartRepair=<Byte Index> Mapping of the output byte for repair on the fieldbus
(>=5)
InputStartRepair=<Byte Index> Mapping of the input byte for repair on the fieldbus (>=5)

Note on repair function
A repair (additional purchase option) always requires a fresh run-through of the entire
robot motion program.

Note
There must be no difference in the robot path, speed and distance to the applicator
head between the teach-in run, first application and repair run.

You will find further information on this in:

Defining the repair area 235 .

text:
Special specifications – optional / Repair interface (licensed option)

8.2.1 Defining the repair area

Note
The repair function is an optional enhancement of RTVision.t and must be enabled
separately. The programming of the communication interface in the higher-level
controller must also be extended. The associated Repair mode tab is only visible if
the repair function is enabled.

Note
The repair is only possible if the function is active for the application, product,
inspection and area. This allows the repair to be easily controlled from the
superordinate level. The repair settings for the area properties impose further
restrictions in terms of repairability.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.2.1 Defining the repair area

text:
In special cases, a component that returns a NOK result can be upgraded to OK through
subsequent automatic reworking (repair) and repeat inspection. However, this option is only
available if the error can be fixed by reapplying glue. For example, gaps in a glue bead can be
touched up but position errors and violations of the maximum glue width cannot be repaired.
The system remembers the position of the faults and can rework them in a second run.

You can activate the repair function for the selected area on the Repair mode tab. The
following settings can also be made:

Operating manual RTVision.t 235
V. 4.8

text:
Special specifications – optional / Repair interface (licensed option)

Relative error tolerance
The error and warning limit values for maximum glue width specified in Error definition 108 can
be increased here for the inspection during the repair run.

Minimal length of the glue gap
Defines the minimum length for categorizing a glue gap as repairable. If this box is not
checked, a glue gap will be categorized as always repairable.

Maximal width of the seam
Defines the maximum width for categorizing a width error as repairable. If this box is not
checked, a width error will be categorized as always repairable.

Note
The Maximal width of the seam setting is only valid for glue beads that are too thin.

Time delay start point time
Defines an offset value in the ms/mm range that moves the start time of the repair to
compensate for any delays in the signal flow.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.2.1 Defining the repair area

text:
Time delay end point
Defines an offset value in the ms/mm range that moves the end time of the repair to
compensate for any delays in the signal flow.

You will find further information on this in:

How does the repair interface work? 233

text:
Special specifications – optional / Interface extension

8.3 Interface extension

The basic interface comprises 4 standard bytes in each case and can be extended through
the addition of further bytes to create a customized interface.
Some options are provided as an example here, which have been implemented to varying
degrees to date together with customers.

Note - special specifications
This involves the implementation of special specifications, which are to be defined
between the operator and the manufacturer BEFORE commissioning.
Depending on the request, the interface can only be extended in consultation with
development and the customer specification; it usually involves the manufacturer
adapting the application, rather than arbitrary adjustments on site by the service
department.

Robot path (only on request):
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.3 Interface extension

text:
Robot path (only on request):

The standard RTVision.t system is a time-based inspection system, whose basic unit is
generally based on [ms]. A path-based approach can be followed here by using the robot
coordinates provided and communication via n bytes. Here too: This special specification
must be defined and programmed in advance; it cannot be integrated as standard.

Inspection result query:
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.3 Interface extension

text:
Inspection result query:

After a component (product) has been inspected, the results of the individual inspections can
be queried (OK, WARN, NOK). After activating the functionality, communication must be
configured via the extended interface using n bytes (usually 5). Once this is done, each
inspection can be assigned its own inspection ID in configuration mode. The inspection result
query can only be issued when the product result is available, it is recognized as valid and the
query is then executed according to the inspection ID.
Here too: This special specification must be defined and configured in advance; it cannot be
integrated as standard.

Take over seam numbers:
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.3 Interface extension

text:
Take over seam numbers:

The extended interface offers the possibility to define n bytes, in which transmitted or stored
seam numbers are taken over and processed internally by the image processing system.
Optional specific settings must be made in the system for this purpose by the authorized
service partner. See Take over seam numbers 242

Transferring serial numbers:

The extended interface offers the possibility to define n bytes, which include transmitted serial
numbers in the result data.
Optional specific settings must be made in the system for this purpose by the authorized
service partner.
The data type (ASCII, hexadecimal or integers) must be defined, while the start byte for the
serial number and the maximum length of the serial number must likewise be specified.

Operating manual RTVision.t 237
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.3 Interface extension

[/TABLE]

text:
Special specifications – optional / Interface extension

Customer data:

The existing basic interface can be extended depending on the type and scope of the
customer data to be received, transferred or made available. The objective, data type(s) and
data length must be defined beforehand. Different numbers of bytes and differently named
bytes can be used for this purpose, so that here, too, a special specification must be defined
in advance. Activation or integration via the authorized service partner is not possible without
prior consultation and, if necessary, adjustment by the manufacturer.

Status of inspection system - glue controller:
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.3 Interface extension

text:
Status of inspection system - glue controller:

With one byte each on the input and output side, signals can be communicated between the
inspection system and glue controller to a limited extent.
It cannot be configured by default, but requires additional programming effort for both
applications. See Communicating with the glue controller 240

See also section:

Communicating with the glue controller 240
Take over seam numbers 242
Data transfer via MQTT transfer protocol 244

238 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8

text:
Special specifications – optional / Communicating with the glue controller

8.4 Communicating with the glue controller

RTVision.t communicates with the glue controller used via the extended interface link.
Depending on the configuration of the corresponding software program, signals can be
exchanged and, if necessary, communicated with the aid of color-coded signaling.

This option has to be specified in advance however and cannot be configured subsequently
on the installed RTVision.t system.

By default, the RTVision.t is the higher-level communication partner in this case.
This is visualized on the software interface on the server screen with an appropriately labeled
button.

This button allows a direct switch from the RTVision.t server to the glue controller software
and indicates (depending on the glue controller software used) basic messages by a change
of color.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.4 Communicating with the glue controller

text:
Button Meaning Action

Grey No communication exists with glue Establish communication - initial
controller communication will be established
at startup.

Green Communication OK No warning or error signal detected

Orange There is a warning from the glue Verification of the data by the glue
controller controller is advisable.

Flashing red An error occurred on the part of the Switch immediately to the glue
glue controller controller software to inspect the
source of the error.

240 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8

based on the coloring of the switch button.

Note
The implementation with color signals always involves additional programming effort
and must be coordinated in detail in advance between the provider of the glue
controller, the operator and the manufacturer.
It should not be set up by default.

text:
Special specifications – optional / Take over seam numbers

8.5 Take over seam numbers

To create a new inspection, click Add. A new entry with a default name in the defined local
language appears in the list of inspections.

Note
Unlike products, inspections are not numbered via the robot controller. They are
always executed in the order in which they appear in the list of inspections. More
information here 65 .

This specific requirement allows the seam numbers to be transferred here as inspection
names.

The Take over seam numbers button below the inspection list is active if the associated
image sequence has been loaded.

242 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8

hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.5 Take over seam numbers

text:
Special specifications – optional / Take over seam numbers

Having acknowledged the confirmation prompt, the seam numbers that were added with the
image sequences in the server are transferred as inspection names.
A seam number is linked with the sequence and transferred from the robot controller for each
image sequence.

Note
Note that for parameterization in RTVision.t, the system must already be definitively
functional. Robot speed, glue control and travel paths must be installed as defined in
the production conditions so that final parameterization of the products can take
place in RTVision.t. If the robot speed is changed, for example, following
parameterization, the product must be taught in again, reparameterized if necessary
and the error map adapted to the new start positions of the areas.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.5 Take over seam numbers

text:
On the server side, a message below the inspection result (in Simple statistics) indicates the
extent to which the seam number assigned to the image sequence matches the seam
number originally transferred.
The message is only displayed if deviations are determined in the seam number; however,
this information is not part of the inspection and does not influence the inspection result.

Operating manual RTVision.t 243
V. 4.8

text:
Special specifications – optional / Data transfer via MQTT

8.6 Data transfer via MQTT

The RTVision.t system allows a number of files to be provided for loading on / collection by
the customer's MQTT system thanks to its comprehensive interface extension.
The program provided separately for this purpose monitors the data generated by RTVision.t
at directory level, extracts the required information and sends it to the predefined server.
This extended data storage allows the data requested by the customer to be made available
for MQTT (using the MQ Telemetry Transport) via a simple dialog.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.6 Data transfer via MQTT

text:
As an external auxiliary tool, this product is reserved for the service department and is
delivered with German and English interface only.
Additional programming effort is likewise needed for RTVision.t to provide the data, which
means that a standard procedure is not used here.
Hence, the previous definition of how the data transfer works has to be defined together in
advance.

You will find further information on this in:

Specific interface extension 237 as a special specification

244 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.6 Data transfer via MQTT

text:
Special specifications – optional / Additional unlicensed specifications

8.7 Additional unlicensed specifications

The specifications outlined here are unlicensed and can be activated via the authorized
service partner.
Note that provision or transfer of data may nonetheless also be required on the part of the
operator to ensure successful use of the option.

Tip - Clarify the desired configuration together in advance
Prior to commissioning therefore, discuss your desired configuration with the
unlicensed specifications.

Ghost mode
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.7 Additional unlicensed specifications

text:
Ghost mode

Ghost mode is an important tool for local commissioning staff because important system
functions, particularly for communication and hardware, can be tested without products
needing to be parameterized fully. Results are output that show the correct system
functionality, though they are not recorded in the statistics data. This means that the image
processing system has no impact on the production process during ghost mode. The

applicable icon in this case is shown in large form in automatic mode (server) when the
mode is active. See Ghost mode 247

Optional database usage
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.7 Additional unlicensed specifications

text:
Optional database usage

The data can be transferred optionally from the directory system to the PostgreSQL
database, which is supplied on request.
Its use is not necessary for single systems. This is also the case with networked systems,
but if there is a requirement for database storage then this option is possible from V.4.8.
See Optional database usage 252

Electronic access control
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.7 Additional unlicensed specifications

text:
Electronic access control

RTVision.t can verify and accept access authorization via electronic key systems (e.g.
Euchner key).
No entry is needed in the user management in this case.
However, if access to the software is required independently of an Electronic Key System
(EKS) for security reasons (for example, in the case of a deputizing arrangement), the
employee should be managed in the user management with their access authorization and
password assignment as a precaution, as otherwise there is no possibility to access
RTVision.t without the Electronic Key System.

Explorer button (server)

On the right of the toolbar at the top, an additional button can be displayed in the server
module if required, which can be used to open Windows Explorer with password protection.
This is a good way to open Explorer specifically without a keyboard at touch-operated
workstations.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.7 Additional unlicensed specifications

text:
Operating manual RTVision.t 245
V. 4.8

Prior to commissioning therefore, discuss your desired configuration with the

[/TABLE]
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.7 Additional unlicensed specifications

text:
Special specifications – optional / Additional unlicensed specifications

This option can also be set by the authorized service partner without a license.

Showing comments (server)

This comment window is offered for text input when the application is saved in configuration
mode.
The comment window can be opened here for viewing under View | Display comment for the
application currently loaded in automatic mode (server).

UI interface - Choosing between two different layouts

The RTVision.t software has been redesigned with regard to its appearance and now offers a
modern work area in two different color layouts for clear handling.
The switchover is carried out in the system after commissioning by the authorized service
partner.

Central language setting
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.7 Additional unlicensed specifications

text:
Central language setting

By activating the relevant variable, the language setting for the installed RTVision.t server,
client and viewer modules can also be made here globally.
Once activated, each module to be opened adopts the language setting of the first module
opened, which means that there is no need to change the language individually any more.
Please contact the authorized service partner in this regard.

Default setting for full screen and window mode

Depending on requirements, full screen or scalable window mode can be set by means of a
variable for working in production.
If window mode is preferred, the module opens and always positions itself at the same
positions and in the sizes as when previously closed.
Please contact the authorized service partner in this regard.

246 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.7 Additional unlicensed specifications

text:
Special specifications – optional / Additional unlicensed specifications

8.7.1 Ghost mode

Ghost mode plays an important role in the course of on-site commissioning.
The system functions are tested with regard to
·
Communication between the glue controller and RTVision.t,
·
Hardware signals,
·
Parameter test for target check (sensor comparison).
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.7.1 Ghost mode

text:
Ghost mode runs without being linked to the other production processes and can be activated
without a product already having been created and taught in.
The result can be ERROR or OK. If the status of communication and hardware is OK, an OK
is always displayed as a result in the RTVision.t server.
This OK does not refer to a product / component. Existing statistics data is therefore not
influenced. All component testing is bypassed in Ghost mode and a result of OK is output,
which is not recorded via the statistics.
The extent to which the three tested components are faulty, missing or functioning perfectly in
terms of their signaling is indicated by a red or green status signal in the Commissioning
dialog. The status is displayed here.
In addition, it is possible to output these statuses with a date and time stamp using a pdf short
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.7.1 Ghost mode

text:
In addition, it is possible to output these statuses with a date and time stamp using a pdf short
report. These reports are saved under Report | Service.
With the result OK, all technologies including the RTVision.tsystem are ready to run and
active at the end of commissioning – a proven means, for example, to bypass the image
processing inspection system temporarily in the course of performance tests, without
switching it off.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.7.1 Ghost mode

text:
Note – Installation of hardware complete – Verification in ghost mode
Assuming that the RTVision.t system is fully installed and connected with its
hardware, both the RTVision.t server and client modules should be started.
At this point, no product (not even sensor comparison/target check) need have been
created as yet. Lack of parameterization does not lead to an error in ghost mode on
the server.

Procedure for activating ghost mode

1. Click Connect to establish a connection from the client to the server. This is a
prerequisite for making further settings on the server.

2. Two of the three inspection options, i.e. Hardware and Communication, are also
displayed with regard to the current status in the status bar on the right-hand side of the
server.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.7.1 Ghost mode

text:
If a problem is already apparent here, it will also exist in the later Commissioning dialog.
That's because this dialog also only shows the states of the hardware and the
communication with color coding.

3. Go to the main menu in the RTVision.t server and select Service | Commissioning. If
the connections are still incomplete, this is immediately displayed in the program status
area:

Operating manual RTVision.t 247
V. 4.8

hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.7.1 Ghost mode

text:
Special specifications – optional / Additional unlicensed specifications

4. If these factors are green, ghost mode can usually result in an OK in the server without
any problems. The mode can also be activated for red factors, but an error is output after
activation in the event of faulty communication or hardware.

Fig.: Two constellations lead to different results on the server

5. Click Activate to activate ghost mode on the server with password protection and
administrator rights only.

248 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.7.1 Ghost mode

text:
Special specifications – optional / Additional unlicensed specifications

The ghost symbols are displayed in the server view when automatic mode is active in a
clearly recognizable way. If all components are still okay, the result is OK. However, no
result from ghost mode is included in the overall statistics.

Note – Ghost mode also outside commissioning
Ghost mode is not only useful during commissioning. Since no results from
component tests are changed, ghost mode can also be activated during various
performance tests, for example. The image processing system does not have to
be deactivated, actual results are not falsified and at the same time the
functioning of signal communication is checked with respect to the above-
mentioned factors.

text:
Special specifications – optional / Additional unlicensed specifications

Interpretation of results

An error is output if there is a hardware error within the image processing system or if
there are problems with communication between the inspection system and the glue
controller.

The corresponding message is also recorded in the logbook.

Special case of sensor comparison

The sensor comparison is a specific product created on the client that performs a target
check using a special referencing plate by means of native parameterization.

Ghost mode now checks whether this functionality is already set up, but also whether it
may have led to a NOK.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.7.1 Ghost mode

text:
Ghost mode now checks whether this functionality is already set up, but also whether it
may have led to a NOK.

The status Ready or Not ready is checked here. Both lead to an OK in the ghost
process, since the red traffic light signal should not lead to an interruption or stop in the
system during the sensor comparison. It is purely a question here of querying the status
of the "Target" product.

250 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.7.1 Ghost mode

text:

Commissioning report

Click Create report to finally create a commissioning report, which is stored in the
Report / Service folder as a pdf file called Commissioning_Date_Time.pdf.

This report shows the status of the three factors mentioned and includes a date and time
stamp.

Deactivate ghost mode to revert to the regular test sequence for the components.

You will find further information on this in:

Special specification of target check / sensor comparison 226

Operating manual RTVision.t 251
V. 4.8
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.7.1 Ghost mode

text:
Special specifications – optional / Additional unlicensed specifications

8.7.2 Optional database usage

RTVision.t generally uses data storage solely as a file system structure.
Now for the first time, Version 4.8 also offers a free option for transferring and storing data in
a database via the file system.
The database storage feature is useful in the case of network integration of multiple systems.
The database can be filled with all actual and target data from the image processing software,
but image sequences cannot be stored there.
In the case of single systems, the database does not offer any real advantage, rather you can
work purely via the directory storage as usual.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 8 Special specifications – optional > 8.7.2 Optional database usage

text:
Note - Database connection
The configuration may only be carried out by the authorized service partner, as the
system must be configured at several points with in-depth settings. Before
commissioning, clarify the data storage you intend to use with the commissioning
engineer.

Tools are provided by the manufacturer for the configuration:
RTVision.t Server
RTVision.t Client
RTVision.t Viewer
RTVision.t Publish (additional program)
PostgreSQL DB (Third party product)

The Publish program provided separately for this purpose monitors the data generated by
RTVision.t at directory level, extracts the required information and sends it to the predefined
server.
The database is installed there and the link between server and database is also set up via
the additional Publish program.

252 Operating manual RTVision.t
V. 4.8

text:
Special specifications – optional / Additional unlicensed specifications

This additional program therefore configures the directory monitoring, the transfer and also
the clean-up of the data in the database.
If the Login to the server button is grayed out, the connection between database and server
is established.
By clicking Close, the additional program is not exited, rather the configuration dialog is
simply closed and the program continues to run in the background.
If the additional program is to be exited, the list of hidden icons should be opened via the task
bar.

Right-clicking the program icon opens the menu for exiting RTVision.t Publish.

Operating manual RTVision.t 253
V. 4.8

Chapter 9
Appendix
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 9 Appendix > General

text:
Appendix / Care and maintenance

9 Appendix

9.1 Care and maintenance

Note - Material damage
The RTVision.t system must be cleaned and inspected regularly to ensure proper
operation.
Servicing and maintenance work where the sensor head is accessed is possible only
during a break in production.
Do not change the position of the sensor to avoid having to readjust the application
parameters.
While cleaning, do not damage the special transparent screen of the sensor (e.g. fine
scratches).

Cleaning the fan filters (computer)

Due to air exchange, over time the filter mats of the PC fan can become very dirty and must
be cleaned at regular intervals.
To do this, open the lock on the front of the computer and then open the two covers. Inside
the covers you will see a fan filter which you should clean with a vacuum cleaner.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 9 Appendix > 9.1 Care and maintenance

text:
the manufacturer is that you check for dirt every week. The level of dirt, however, depends
very much on the surrounding conditions. We would therefore ask that the intervals be
adjusted according to prior experience with maintenance.

Cleaning the sensor

Note
Normally there is no need to clean the lens in RTVision.t because of its design.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 9 Appendix > 9.1 Care and maintenance

text:
Cleaning the sensor

Note
Normally there is no need to clean the lens in RTVision.t because of its design.

The lenses have a special surface coat to prevent mirroring and reflections. A dirty surface on
the lenses or other parts in the optical path caused by dust, oil or grease creates shadowing
effects. The uniform imaging of the objects is consequently impaired. The lenses are
protected behind a special acrylic glass screen, which means that regular cleaning of this
screen is generally sufficient.
Effects of dirt on the sensor surface are:
·
Over time, the recorded images become continuously darker
·
Dark points in some areas of the image
·
Over time, the recorded images become blurred

the manufacturer is that you check for dirt every week or always if one of the described
phenomena occurs.

V. 4.8

text:
Appendix / Care and maintenance

Note - Material damage
Make sure that the transparent special screen of the sensor is not damaged.
Scratches can cause changes in the optical properties or even a complete
breakdown.
Do not rub with a dry cloth or clean with chemical solvents. Avoid all cleaning
products that could damage the acrylic glass surface.
When wiping, avoid applying too much pressure to the surface.

Cleaning process:
·
Use a soft, oil-free paintbrush to remove dust from the sensor surface.
·
To remove oil, grease or fingerprints from the sensor surface, wipe the dirty area with a
moist clean soft cloth and some household cleaning product and dry the surface. Use a
microfiber cloth or an oil-free, clean soft cotton cloth.
·
Repeat the wiping process until the surface of the sensor's cover screen is completely
clean.

text:
Appendix / Care and maintenance

9.1.1 Replacing the sensor cover

Danger of electric shock!
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 9 Appendix > 9.1.1 Replacing the sensor cover

text:
·
Ensure that work can be carried out in accordance with occupational safety.
·
This work may only be carried out by trained maintenance staff or other authorized
personnel during a production break when the robot arm is not moving.
·
Following the orderly shutting down of the 28 image recording software RTVision.t
make sure to switch off the strobe controller.
·
Check that there is no voltage present in the system.
·
When the strobe controller has been switched off, disconnect the cable on the sensor
head.
The sensor optics are protected from environmental influences by an acrylic glass cover
screen. Depending on the sensor version, it can also perform the function of a polarizing filter
and/or a diffuser. When changing ensure the functionality of the cover screen is identical to
the one being replaced.
Check the scope of delivery for the replacement screen immediately after delivery.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 9 Appendix > 9.1.1 Replacing the sensor cover

text:
the one being replaced.
Check the scope of delivery for the replacement screen immediately after delivery.
Components include screen with seal and O-ring.
hierarchy_path: QUISS 2D RTV.t V4.8_Operation_Manual_EN > 9 Appendix > 9.1.1 Replacing the sensor cover

text:
As this cover screen is dirtied and therefore also scratched by the production environment, it
should be checked regularly and changed as required.

Note - Material damage!
To remove and replace the cover screen you will require a pair of antistatic gloves
(soft material) and an O-ring hook tool to remove the O-ring undamaged from the
groove.
Make sure that the transparent special screen of the sensor is not damaged.
Scratches can cause changes in the optical properties or even a complete
breakdown. Work with gloves to minimize the risk of damaging the new cover screen.

1. Maintenance work with access to the sensor head is possible only during a break in
production.

2. When the application has been shut down, 28 switch off the LED light controller (strobe
controller).

3. Then disconnect the cable on the sensor head.

Operating manual RTVision.t 257
V. 4.8
